question: Which employees manage customers with the highest combined invoice totals?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: What is the total invoice amount billed in each city?
How many customers does each support representative manage, grouped by employee city?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.city AS employee_city, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.city
ORDER BY e.city;

question: Which artists have albums that include tracks from more than three different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name AS artist_name
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track tr ON al.album_id = tr.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT tr.genre_id) > 3;

question: Find the employees who manage customers with the largest total number of invoices over the last year.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(inv_count) AS total_invoices
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN (
    SELECT customer_id, COUNT(invoice_id) AS inv_count
    FROM invoice
    WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
    GROUP BY customer_id
) inv ON c.customer_id = inv.customer_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING SUM(inv_count) = (
    SELECT MAX(total_inv) FROM (
        SELECT SUM(inv_count) AS total_inv
        FROM employee e2
        JOIN customer c2 ON e2.employee_id = c2.support_rep_id
        JOIN (
            SELECT customer_id, COUNT(invoice_id) AS inv_count
            FROM invoice
            WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
            GROUP BY customer_id
        ) inv2 ON c2.customer_id = inv2.customer_id
        GROUP BY e2.employee_id
    ) sub
)

question: What is the average track price and total number of tracks available grouped by genre and media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, mt.name AS media_type_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_track_price, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY g.name, mt.name
ORDER BY g.name, mt.name;

question: What are the average track durations grouped by album and artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.title AS album_title, ar.name AS artist_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration_milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
GROUP BY a.title, ar.name
ORDER BY ar.name, a.title;

question: What is the total number of tracks purchased by customers from Canada?
gold_sql:
SELECT SUM(il.quantity) AS total_tracks_purchased
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
WHERE c.country = 'Canada';

question: Which employees support customers who have bought tracks from the Rock genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name = 'Rock'

question: List the invoices with the highest total amounts and include the customer and billing city for each.
gold_sql:
SELECT i.invoice_id, i.total, c.first_name, c.last_name, i.billing_city
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE i.total = (SELECT MAX(total) FROM invoice)
ORDER BY i.invoice_id;

question: For each support representative, what is the average spending of their customers compared to the overall customer average?
gold_sql:
WITH SupportRepAvg AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS support_rep_name,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_spending_per_customer
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, support_rep_name
),
OverallAvg AS (
 SELECT
 AVG(i.total) AS overall_avg_spending
 FROM invoice i
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 )

SELECT
 s.employee_id,
 s.support_rep_name,
 s.avg_spending_per_customer,
 o.overall_avg_spending,
 s.avg_spending_per_customer / o.overall_avg_spending AS spending_ratio
FROM SupportRepAvg s
CROSS JOIN OverallAvg o
ORDER BY s.support_rep_name;

question: How many albums by each artist have an average track price above the overall average?
gold_sql:
WITH OverallAvgPrice AS (
  SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS avg_price
  FROM track
),
AlbumAvgPrice AS (
  SELECT album_id, AVG(unit_price) AS avg_album_price
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
AlbumsAboveAvg AS (
  SELECT album_id
  FROM AlbumAvgPrice, OverallAvgPrice
  WHERE avg_album_price > avg_price
)
SELECT ar.artist_id, ar.name, COUNT(a.album_id) AS albums_above_avg
FROM artist ar
JOIN album a ON ar.artist_id = a.artist_id
JOIN AlbumsAboveAvg aaa ON a.album_id = aaa.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY albums_above_avg DESC;

question: Can you identify playlists that have a higher average track duration than the overall average track duration?
gold_sql:
WITH overall_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_duration
  FROM track
),
playlist_avg AS (
  SELECT pt.playlist_id, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, pa.avg_duration
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_avg pa ON p.playlist_id = pa.playlist_id
JOIN overall_avg oa ON pa.avg_duration > oa.avg_duration;

question: What are the total invoice amounts by billing postal code and how do they rank?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_postal_code, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(total) DESC) AS rank
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_postal_code
ORDER BY rank;

question: Among employees who manage support representatives, how many reports does each manager have on average?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 AVG(report_count) AS avg_reports_per_manager
FROM (
 SELECT
 manager.employee_id,
 COUNT(report.employee_id) AS report_count
 FROM employee AS manager
 JOIN employee AS report ON report.reports_to = manager.employee_id
 GROUP BY manager.employee_id
) AS manager_reports;

question: How many artists are listed in the catalog?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS artist_count FROM artist;

question: How much revenue was generated in the year 2023?
Which playlists have tracks that appear in the greatest number of other playlists as well?
gold_sql:
-- Step 1: For each track, count how many distinct playlists it appears in
WITH TrackPlaylistCounts AS (
    SELECT track_id, COUNT(DISTINCT playlist_id) AS playlist_count
    FROM playlist_track
    GROUP BY track_id
),

-- Step 2: For each playlist, calculate the sum of the counts of other playlists each track appears in (excluding the current playlist)
PlaylistTrackOverlap AS (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id, pt.track_id, (tpc.playlist_count - 1) AS other_playlists_count
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN TrackPlaylistCounts tpc ON pt.track_id = tpc.track_id
),

-- Step 3: Aggregate by playlist to get the total overlap count
PlaylistOverlapSum AS (
    SELECT playlist_id, SUM(other_playlists_count) AS total_other_playlists_count
    FROM PlaylistTrackOverlap
    GROUP BY playlist_id
),

-- Step 4: Find the maximum overlap count
MaxOverlap AS (
    SELECT MAX(total_other_playlists_count) AS max_overlap
    FROM PlaylistOverlapSum
)

-- Step 5: Select playlists with the maximum overlap count
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, pos.total_other_playlists_count
FROM PlaylistOverlapSum pos
JOIN MaxOverlap mo ON pos.total_other_playlists_count = mo.max_overlap
JOIN playlist p ON pos.playlist_id = p.playlist_id;

question: List the employees along with the count of their direct reports and indirect reports combined.
gold_sql:
WITH RECURSIVE EmployeeReports AS (
    SELECT employee_id, reports_to AS manager_id
    FROM employee
    WHERE reports_to IS NOT NULL
    UNION ALL
    SELECT e.employee_id, er.manager_id
    FROM employee e
    JOIN EmployeeReports er ON e.reports_to = er.employee_id
)
SELECT 
    e.employee_id,
    e.first_name,
    e.last_name,
    COUNT(er.employee_id) AS total_reports
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN EmployeeReports er ON e.employee_id = er.manager_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which support representative handles customers with the highest total invoice amounts?
gold_sql:
WITH total_by_rep AS (
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 WHERE e.title LIKE '%Support%'
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
)
SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, total_invoice_amount
FROM total_by_rep
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Identify employees supporting customers with the widest range of invoice totals.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, MIN(i.total) AS min_invoice_total, MAX(i.total) AS max_invoice_total, MAX(i.total) - MIN(i.total) AS invoice_total_range
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY invoice_total_range DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the rank of each playlist by the number of distinct albums represented in its tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) AS distinct_album_count,
       RANK() OVER (ORDER BY COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) DESC) AS album_rank
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY album_rank;

question: How many employees have hired more than five other employees reporting directly to them?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS employees_with_more_than_five_reports
FROM (
 SELECT reports_to, COUNT(employee_id) AS direct_reports
 FROM employee
 GROUP BY reports_to
 HAVING COUNT(employee_id) > 5
) AS subquery;

question: List employees who do not have any customers assigned to them.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
WHERE c.customer_id IS NULL;

question: What are the top five albums with the longest total track duration?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(t.milliseconds) AS total_duration
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY total_duration DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Rank customers by their total spending
gold_sql:
SELECT
                c.first_name, c.last_name,
                SUM(i.total) as total_spent,
                RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(i.total) DESC) as spending_rank
            FROM customer c
            JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
            GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
            ORDER BY spending_rank

question: Which genres have the highest number of tracks priced above $1.00?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS tracks_above_1dollar
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE t.unit_price > 1.00
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY tracks_above_1dollar DESC;

question: What is the total invoice amount generated by customers assigned to each support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: How many playlists are created in total?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_playlists FROM playlist;

question: What are the most popular genres by number of tracks sold?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name, COUNT(il.track_id) as tracks_sold
            FROM invoice_line il
            JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
            ORDER BY tracks_sold DESC
            LIMIT 10

question: How does the total spending of customers vary by state?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.state, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.state
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: Which playlists include tracks from artists whose albums span multiple genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
JOIN (
    SELECT a.artist_id
    FROM album a
    JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY a.artist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
) multi_genre_artists ON ar.artist_id = multi_genre_artists.artist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name;

question: What is the distribution of albums by artist along with the average track duration per artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT a.album_id) AS album_count,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_duration_seconds
FROM album a
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY album_count DESC;

question: What is the average total amount spent on invoices by customers in each country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, AVG(i.total) AS average_total_spent
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.country
ORDER BY c.country;

question: Which albums have the largest total size of tracks in bytes?
gold_sql:
SELECT album.album_id, album.title, SUM(track.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM album album
JOIN track track ON album.album_id = track.album_id
GROUP BY album.album_id, album.title
ORDER BY total_bytes DESC;

question: List all customers who have never purchased any tracks from the Classical genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
FROM customer c
WHERE c.customer_id NOT IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT i.customer_id
    FROM invoice i
    JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
    JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
    JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
    WHERE g.name = 'Classical'
    AND i.customer_id IS NOT NULL
)

question: What is the average number of tracks per playlist?
Which genre contains the most tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the distribution of the number of tracks per playlist across the entire database?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  track_count,
  COUNT(*) AS playlist_count,
  COUNT(*) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM playlist) AS percentage
FROM (
  SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist_track
  GROUP BY playlist_id
) AS track_counts
GROUP BY track_count
ORDER BY track_count;

question: For each media type, what is the average track size in bytes and total number of tracks?
gold_sql:

FROM media_type mt
JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY mt.name;

question: How many tracks are available in each media type, grouped by genre?
gold_sql:

JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY mt.name, g.name

question: How many customers have a support representative assigned?
Identify employees who have been hired before 2010 and the number of customers assigned to them.
gold_sql:

FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
WHERE e.hire_date < '2010-01-01'
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name

question: Who are the employees that do not report to anyone within the company?

FROM employee e

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: Which playlists contain the longest tracks in terms of duration?

JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.milliseconds = (SELECT MAX(milliseconds) FROM track);

question: What is the average duration of tracks included in each playlist?

question: How many different media types are available?

question: Find playlists that contain tracks with unit prices above the average track price across all albums.
gold_sql:
WITH avg_price AS (
    SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
    FROM track
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN avg_price ap ON t.unit_price > ap.avg_unit_price;

question: Find playlists where the total size in bytes of tracks exceeds the average playlist size.
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistSizes AS (
  SELECT pt.playlist_id, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
),
AverageSize AS (
  SELECT AVG(total_bytes) AS avg_bytes
  FROM PlaylistSizes
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, ps.total_bytes
FROM playlist p
JOIN PlaylistSizes ps ON p.playlist_id = ps.playlist_id
JOIN AverageSize a ON ps.total_bytes > a.avg_bytes;

question: What is the average invoice total for customers assigned to each support representative?

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: What is the shortest track available in the catalog?

question: How many employees report to each manager, and what is the average total invoice amount for customers supported by those employees?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 m.employee_id AS manager_id,
 m.first_name || ' ' || m.last_name AS manager_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS num_employees_reporting,
 AVG(COALESCE(inv_totals.total_invoice_amount, 0)) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM employee m
LEFT JOIN employee e ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
LEFT JOIN customer c ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
LEFT JOIN (
 SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY customer_id
) inv_totals ON inv_totals.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY m.employee_id, m.first_name, m.last_name
ORDER BY num_employees_reporting DESC;

question: Which playlists include tracks longer than the average track duration in their respective genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN (
    SELECT genre_id, AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_duration
    FROM track
    GROUP BY genre_id
) avg_genre ON t.genre_id = avg_genre.genre_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > avg_genre.avg_duration;

question: How many employees report to each manager and what is the average invoice total from their customers?

SELECT

 AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee m
LEFT JOIN employee e ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
LEFT JOIN customer c ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY m.employee_id, m.first_name, m.last_name

question: Which employees have the highest number of customers assigned to them, and what is the total invoice amount generated by those customers?
gold_sql:

FROM employee e

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) = (
 SELECT MAX(customer_count) FROM (
 SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT c2.customer_id) AS customer_count
 FROM employee e2

question: How many albums have been released by each artist and what is the average number of tracks per album?

FROM album a
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT album_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM track
    GROUP BY album_id
) t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, ar.name

question: Which customers have increased their spending by more than 20% compared to the previous year?
gold_sql:
WITH yearly_spending AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY') AS year,
 SUM(total) AS total_spent
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY customer_id, year
),
spending_comparison AS (
 SELECT
 current.customer_id,
 current.year AS current_year,
 current.total_spent AS current_spent,
 previous.total_spent AS previous_spent,
 (current.total_spent - previous.total_spent) / previous.total_spent::numeric AS spending_increase
 FROM yearly_spending current
 JOIN yearly_spending previous ON current.customer_id = previous.customer_id
WHERE CAST(current.year AS INTEGER) = CAST(previous.year AS INTEGER) + 1
)
SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name,
 sc.current_year,
 sc.current_spent,
 sc.previous_spent,
 sc.spending_increase
FROM spending_comparison sc
JOIN customer c ON sc.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE sc.spending_increase > 0.20
ORDER BY sc.spending_increase DESC;

question: Which employees support customers with the highest average monthly invoice totals?

JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: For each artist, what is the difference between the duration of their longest and shortest track?

       MAX(t.milliseconds) - MIN(t.milliseconds) AS duration_difference_milliseconds
FROM artist a

JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id

question: What is the average spending per customer?
Which genres have the smallest average file size in bytes for their tracks?

ORDER BY avg_file_size_bytes ASC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the proportion of tracks by genre within each album, and which albums have the most diverse genre representation?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreCounts AS (
  SELECT
    t.album_id,
    t.genre_id,
    COUNT(*) AS genre_track_count
  FROM track t
  GROUP BY t.album_id, t.genre_id
),
AlbumCounts AS (
  SELECT
    album_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_tracks
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
GenreProportions AS (
  SELECT
    gc.album_id,
    gc.genre_id,
    gc.genre_track_count,
    ac.total_tracks,
    CAST(gc.genre_track_count AS FLOAT) / ac.total_tracks AS genre_proportion
  FROM GenreCounts gc
  JOIN AlbumCounts ac ON gc.album_id = ac.album_id
),
AlbumGenreDiversity AS (
  SELECT
    album_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id) AS genre_count
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
MaxDiversity AS (
  SELECT MAX(genre_count) AS max_genre_count FROM AlbumGenreDiversity
),
MostDiverseAlbums AS (
  SELECT agd.album_id, agd.genre_count
  FROM AlbumGenreDiversity agd
  JOIN MaxDiversity md ON agd.genre_count = md.max_genre_count
)
SELECT
  a.title AS album_title,
  g.name AS genre_name,
  gp.genre_proportion,
  mda.genre_count AS diversity_count
FROM GenreProportions gp
JOIN album a ON gp.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN genre g ON gp.genre_id = g.genre_id
LEFT JOIN MostDiverseAlbums mda ON gp.album_id = mda.album_id
ORDER BY a.album_id, gp.genre_proportion DESC;

question: What is the total revenue generated by each employee's assigned customers in the last quarter?
gold_sql:
WITH LastQuarter AS (
 SELECT
 CASE
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (1, 2, 3) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT - 1, '-10-01'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (4, 5, 6) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-01-01'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (7, 8, 9) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-04-01'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 ELSE TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-07-01'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 END AS start_date,
 CASE
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (1, 2, 3) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT - 1, '-12-31'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (4, 5, 6) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-03-31'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CURRENT_DATE) IN (7, 8, 9) THEN TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-06-30'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 ELSE TO_DATE(CONCAT(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CURRENT_DATE)::INT, '-09-30'), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
 END AS end_date
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN LastQuarter lq ON i.invoice_date BETWEEN lq.start_date AND lq.end_date
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: How many tracks by each artist have a duration longer than the average duration of all tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  ar.artist_id,
  ar.name AS artist_name,
  COUNT(t.track_id) AS tracks_longer_than_avg
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > (SELECT AVG(milliseconds) FROM track)
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY tracks_longer_than_avg DESC;

question: Identify customers who purchased tracks from the most albums and their total spending on those tracks.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerAlbumPurchases AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) AS distinct_album_count,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
),
MaxAlbumCount AS (
 SELECT MAX(distinct_album_count) AS max_albums FROM CustomerAlbumPurchases
)
SELECT
 cap.customer_id,
 cap.first_name,
 cap.last_name,
 cap.distinct_album_count,
 cap.total_spending
FROM CustomerAlbumPurchases cap
JOIN MaxAlbumCount mac ON cap.distinct_album_count = mac.max_albums
ORDER BY cap.customer_id;

question: What is the monthly spending pattern for customers assigned to each support representative?

 e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS support_rep_name,

 SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, year_month
ORDER BY e.employee_id, year_month;

question: For each customer, how many distinct genres of tracks have they purchased?

question: How many tracks from each genre are included in user-created playlists?

FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id

question: Identify support representatives whose customers have purchased tracks from the largest variety of genres.
gold_sql:
WITH SupportRepGenreCount AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id AS support_rep_id,
 e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS support_rep_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
), MaxGenreCount AS (
 SELECT MAX(genre_count) AS max_genre_count FROM SupportRepGenreCount
)
SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 support_rep_name,
 genre_count
FROM SupportRepGenreCount
WHERE genre_count = (SELECT max_genre_count FROM MaxGenreCount);

question: What is the distribution of total spending among customers grouped by their support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending, 
 SUM(i.total) / (SELECT SUM(total) FROM invoice) AS spending_percentage
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: For each playlist, how many tracks were purchased more than once according to invoice data?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) AS tracks_purchased_more_than_once
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN (
    SELECT track_id
    FROM invoice_line
    GROUP BY track_id
    HAVING SUM(quantity) > 1
) il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the total revenue generated per billing postal code?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_postal_code, SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_postal_code
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: Can you show the reporting hierarchy for employees up to two levels?
gold_sql:
SELECT e1.employee_id AS employee_id_level_1,
 e1.first_name || ' ' || e1.last_name AS employee_name_level_1,
 e1.title AS title_level_1,
 e2.employee_id AS employee_id_level_2,
 e2.first_name || ' ' || e2.last_name AS employee_name_level_2,
 e2.title AS title_level_2,
 e3.employee_id AS employee_id_level_3,
 e3.first_name || ' ' || e3.last_name AS employee_name_level_3,
 e3.title AS title_level_3
FROM employee e1
LEFT JOIN employee e2 ON e2.reports_to = e1.employee_id
LEFT JOIN employee e3 ON e3.reports_to = e2.employee_id
 -- North America region
ORDER BY e1.employee_id, e2.employee_id, e3.employee_id;

question: How many invoices were issued per customer in the last six months and what is the average spending per invoice?
gold_sql:
SELECT customer_id, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoices_count, AVG(total) AS avg_spending_per_invoice
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY customer_id;

question: Can you identify playlists where the average track price is above the overall average track price?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING AVG(t.unit_price) > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track)

question: What is the total quantity of items sold by invoice?
Find the playlists that include tracks composed by more than three different composers.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.composer IS NOT NULL AND t.composer <> ''
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) > 3;

question: How many artists have released more than five albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT artist_id) AS artist_count
FROM (
  SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
  FROM album
  GROUP BY artist_id
  HAVING COUNT(album_id) > 5
) AS artist_album_counts;

question: What is the distribution of customers per support representative by city?
gold_sql:
WITH city_rep_customers AS (
 SELECT e.city AS city,
 e.employee_id AS support_rep_id,
 COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.city, e.employee_id
), city_totals AS (
 SELECT city,
 SUM(customer_count) AS total_customers
 FROM city_rep_customers
 GROUP BY city
)
SELECT crc.city,
 crc.support_rep_id,
 crc.customer_count,
 CAST(crc.customer_count AS FLOAT) / ct.total_customers AS customer_percentage
FROM city_rep_customers crc
JOIN city_totals ct ON crc.city = ct.city
ORDER BY crc.city, customer_percentage DESC;

question: What is the average price of tracks grouped by genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which employees have the largest number of direct reports?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee r ON e.employee_id = r.reports_to
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY direct_reports_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the count of employees by their country of residence?
gold_sql:
SELECT country, COUNT(employee_id) AS employee_count
FROM employee
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY employee_count DESC;

question: Which playlists have no tracks assigned to them?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
LEFT JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
WHERE pt.track_id IS NULL;

question: Which support representatives manage customers with the largest average invoice value?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_value
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_value DESC;

question: Which genre generates the most revenue from sales according to invoice lines?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average age of employees based on their birth dates?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(year FROM AGE(CURRENT_DATE, birth_date))) AS average_age
FROM employee

question: Which billing cities have the highest average invoice amount and how many customers contribute to it?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_amount, COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC;

question: How many invoices have totals less than the average invoice amount?
What is the average track duration per genre, and which genre has the longest average duration?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_duration_seconds
    FROM track t
    JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
    GROUP BY g.name
)
SELECT genre_name, avg_duration_seconds
FROM GenreAvgDuration
WHERE avg_duration_seconds = (SELECT MAX(avg_duration_seconds) FROM GenreAvgDuration);

question: Which customers have made the highest number of purchases by invoice count?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the total spending of each customer on music tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: For each genre, which track has the longest duration and how does that duration compare to the average duration of tracks in that genre?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreTrackDurations AS (
  SELECT
    g.genre_id,
    g.name AS genre_name,
    t.track_id,
    t.name AS track_name,
    t.milliseconds
  FROM genre g
  JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
),
MaxDurationPerGenre AS (
  SELECT
    genre_id,
    MAX(milliseconds) AS max_duration
  FROM GenreTrackDurations
  GROUP BY genre_id
),
AvgDurationPerGenre AS (
  SELECT
    genre_id,
    AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_duration
  FROM GenreTrackDurations
  GROUP BY genre_id
)
SELECT
  gtd.genre_name,
  gtd.track_name,
  gtd.milliseconds AS longest_duration,
  adpg.avg_duration,
  (gtd.milliseconds - adpg.avg_duration) / adpg.avg_duration AS duration_difference_ratio
FROM GenreTrackDurations gtd
JOIN MaxDurationPerGenre mdpg ON gtd.genre_id = mdpg.genre_id AND gtd.milliseconds = mdpg.max_duration
JOIN AvgDurationPerGenre adpg ON gtd.genre_id = adpg.genre_id
ORDER BY gtd.genre_name;

question: What is the average price of a track?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS average_track_price
FROM track;

question: How many playlists were created with no tracks assigned?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_no_tracks
FROM playlist p
LEFT JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
WHERE pt.track_id IS NULL;

question: What is the average total invoice amount for customers managed by each employee?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: What is the sum of invoice totals grouped by support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_sum
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_sum DESC;

question: What is the count and total revenue of invoices for customers grouped by support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count, SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: Which playlists contain more than 100 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.name, COUNT(*) as track_count
            FROM playlist p
            JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
            GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 100
            ORDER BY track_count DESC

question: How many playlists contain tracks from more than five different artists?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_more_than_five_artists
FROM (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
    JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) > 5
) sub;

question: Which customers spent the most in 2023?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(i.total) as total_spent
            FROM customer c
            JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
            WHERE i.invoice_date >= '2023-01-01' AND i.invoice_date < '2024-01-01'
            GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
            ORDER BY total_spent DESC
            LIMIT 10

question: How many customers have made purchases above $100 in total?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS customers_above_100
FROM invoice
WHERE total > 100

question: How many albums are there in the catalog?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS album_count FROM album;

question: Identify employees who support customers from more than three different countries.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) AS distinct_countries
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) > 3;

question: Which artist has the longest total duration of tracks across all their albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name AS artist_name, SUM(t.milliseconds) AS total_duration_milliseconds
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY total_duration_milliseconds DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: How many employees have the title 'Sales Support Agent'?
Which employees support customers with the highest average number of invoices per year?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerInvoicesPerYear AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id AS employee_id,
 c.customer_id,
 EXTRACT(YEAR FROM i.invoice_date) AS invoice_year,
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoices_per_year
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 -- North America countries
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM i.invoice_date)
),
EmployeeAvgInvoices AS (
 SELECT
 employee_id,
 AVG(invoices_per_year) AS avg_invoices_per_year
 FROM CustomerInvoicesPerYear
 GROUP BY employee_id
),
MaxAvgInvoices AS (
 SELECT MAX(avg_invoices_per_year) AS max_avg_invoices
 FROM EmployeeAvgInvoices
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 e.title,
 ea.avg_invoices_per_year
FROM EmployeeAvgInvoices ea
JOIN MaxAvgInvoices m ON ea.avg_invoices_per_year = m.max_avg_invoices
JOIN employee e ON ea.employee_id = e.employee_id;

question: What percentage of all tracks are in the Rock genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT
                ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM track), 2) as rock_percentage
            FROM track t
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            WHERE g.name = 'Rock'

question: For each playlist, what is the count of tracks grouped by media type and the total duration in milliseconds?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, mt.media_type_id, mt.name AS media_type_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, SUM(t.milliseconds) AS total_duration_milliseconds
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name, mt.media_type_id, mt.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id, mt.media_type_id;

question: Find artists whose total track sales revenue exceeds the average revenue of all artists.
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistRevenue AS (
 SELECT ar.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
 FROM artist ar
 JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
 JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
 JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
),
AverageRevenue AS (
 SELECT AVG(total_revenue) AS avg_revenue
 FROM ArtistRevenue
)
SELECT artist_name, total_revenue
FROM ArtistRevenue, AverageRevenue
WHERE total_revenue > avg_revenue
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: How many employees are currently working as support representatives?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS support_representative_count
FROM employee
WHERE title = 'Support Representative'

question: Which billing cities have the highest average invoice totals?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY average_invoice_total DESC;

question: Which playlist includes the track with the longest duration and what is the playlist name?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, t.track_id, t.milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
WHERE t.milliseconds = (SELECT MAX(milliseconds) FROM track);

question: List the top 10 customers by total track purchases in the last quarter.
gold_sql:
SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name,
 COUNT(il.invoice_line_id) AS total_track_purchases
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '3 months'
 AND i.invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('quarter', CURRENT_DATE)
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_track_purchases DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the total number of invoices and average invoice amount for customers grouped by company?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.company, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_amount
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.company
ORDER BY c.company;

question: Which artists have the highest number of tracks priced above the median track price?
gold_sql:
WITH MedianPrice AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS median_price
  FROM track
),
TracksAboveMedian AS (
  SELECT a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name, t.track_id
  FROM track t
  JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
  JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
  CROSS JOIN MedianPrice mp
  WHERE t.unit_price > mp.median_price
)
SELECT artist_id, artist_name, COUNT(track_id) AS tracks_above_median
FROM TracksAboveMedian
GROUP BY artist_id, artist_name
ORDER BY tracks_above_median DESC;

question: Which employees have customers from the highest number of distinct countries?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) AS distinct_countries
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) = (
    SELECT MAX(country_count) FROM (
        SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT country) AS country_count
        FROM customer
        GROUP BY support_rep_id
    ) AS country_counts
)

question: Which artists have released the longest track in each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, a.name AS artist_name, t.name AS track_name, t.milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
WHERE (t.genre_id, t.milliseconds) IN (
    SELECT genre_id, MAX(milliseconds)
    FROM track
    GROUP BY genre_id
)
ORDER BY g.name;

question: What is the average invoice total for customers segmented by billing state?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY billing_state;

question: How many unique genres are represented in the 'classic rock' playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS unique_genres
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE p.name = 'Classic Rock';

question: Find employees who support customers in cities with the highest sales revenue.
gold_sql:
WITH CitySales AS (
 SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_revenue
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city
),
MaxCitySales AS (
 SELECT MAX(total_revenue) AS max_revenue
 FROM CitySales
),
TopCities AS (
 SELECT billing_city
 FROM CitySales
 WHERE total_revenue = (SELECT max_revenue FROM MaxCitySales)
)
SELECT DISTINCT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.title, e.city, e.state, e.country
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN TopCities tc ON c.city = tc.billing_city
 ;

question: Which playlist has the most tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which employees have the most direct reports?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee r ON e.employee_id = r.reports_to
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY direct_reports_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How many tracks have been purchased more than once?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (
                SELECT track_id
                FROM invoice_line
                GROUP BY track_id
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
            ) multi_purchase

question: What genres have tracks that appear in the highest number of different playlists?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) AS playlist_count
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY playlist_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total revenue generated by customers from each city?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.city, SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY c.city
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: List the top 5 customers who have purchased the most tracks by quantity.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_tracks_purchased
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_tracks_purchased DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Which genres have the longest average track lengths, and what is the total number of tracks in these genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, 
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_length_seconds, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_length_seconds DESC;

question: Identify employees who have been working longer than their direct managers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.hire_date AS employee_hire_date, m.employee_id AS manager_id, m.first_name AS manager_first_name, m.last_name AS manager_last_name, m.hire_date AS manager_hire_date
FROM employee e
JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
WHERE e.hire_date < m.hire_date;

question: Identify support representatives with the greatest disparity in invoice totals among their assigned customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 MAX(cust_invoice_totals.total) - MIN(cust_invoice_totals.total) AS invoice_total_disparity
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN (
 SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS total
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY customer_id
) cust_invoice_totals ON c.customer_id = cust_invoice_totals.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY invoice_total_disparity DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: How many customers are assigned to each support representative, and what is the total revenue from their customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS num_customers, COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_revenue
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY num_customers DESC;

question: Show all tracks with their genre name and media type
gold_sql:
SELECT t.name as track, g.name as genre, mt.name as media_type
            FROM track t
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id

question: How many invoices per month were issued to customers from the top three cities by total sales?
gold_sql:
WITH CitySales AS (
 SELECT
 c.city,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_sales
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 -- North America region
 GROUP BY c.city
),
TopCities AS (
 SELECT city
 FROM CitySales
 ORDER BY total_sales DESC
 LIMIT 3
)
SELECT
 TO_CHAR(i.invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN TopCities tc ON c.city = tc.city
 -- North America region
GROUP BY year_month
ORDER BY year_month;

question: What is the total size in bytes of all tracks combined?

question: List all customers from Canada

question: Which playlists contain tracks from more than one genre, and how many such genres do they include?
gold_sql:

FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1;

question: What is the total number of invoices billed to each postal code?

question: How many albums does each artist have on average?
Which billing countries have the highest number of invoices created in the last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count, 
       COUNT(invoice_id) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(invoice_id)) OVER () AS invoice_percentage
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: Identify playlists that contain at least one track not sold in any invoice line.
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
LEFT JOIN invoice_line il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
WHERE il.track_id IS NULL;

question: How many albums does each artist have and which artists have more than five albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name, COUNT(al.album_id) AS album_count
FROM artist a
LEFT JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
HAVING COUNT(al.album_id) > 5;

question: Can you find albums where the total duration of all tracks exceeds the average total duration of all albums?
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumDurations AS (
  SELECT album_id, SUM(milliseconds) AS total_duration
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
AverageDuration AS (
  SELECT AVG(total_duration) AS avg_duration
  FROM AlbumDurations
)
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, ad.total_duration
FROM album a
JOIN AlbumDurations ad ON a.album_id = ad.album_id
JOIN AverageDuration avgd ON ad.total_duration > avgd.avg_duration;

question: List playlists ordered by the total number of distinct genres included in their tracks, showing the top 10.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genre_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY distinct_genre_count DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the average invoice total per month for customers assigned to each employee?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlyTotals AS (
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, 
 EXTRACT(YEAR FROM i.invoice_date) AS year, 
 EXTRACT(MONTH FROM i.invoice_date) AS month, 
 SUM(i.total) AS total_per_month
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, year, month
)
SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, AVG(total_per_month) AS avg_invoice_total_per_month
FROM MonthlyTotals
GROUP BY employee_id, first_name, last_name
ORDER BY employee_id;

question: Identify the playlists containing tracks purchased by customers with the highest spending in the past year.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_id = (
    SELECT c.customer_id
    FROM customer c
    JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
    JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
    WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
    GROUP BY c.customer_id
    ORDER BY SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
AND i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name;

question: How does the total number of albums per artist correlate with the average track price?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  a.artist_id,
  COUNT(DISTINCT al.album_id) AS total_albums,
  AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id;

question: Find artists who have released albums with the highest total track size in bytes.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_track_size_bytes
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
HAVING SUM(t.bytes) = (
    SELECT MAX(total_bytes) FROM (
        SELECT SUM(t2.bytes) AS total_bytes
        FROM artist a2
        JOIN album al2 ON a2.artist_id = al2.artist_id
        JOIN track t2 ON al2.album_id = t2.album_id
        GROUP BY a2.artist_id
    ) sub
)

question: For each support representative, what is the difference between the maximum and minimum invoice totals among their customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, MAX(i.total) - MIN(i.total) AS difference_max_min_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY difference_max_min_invoice_total DESC;

question: What is the average invoice total across all sales?
gold_sql:

FROM invoice;

question: List managers along with the total number of customers supported by their direct and indirect reports.
gold_sql:
WITH RECURSIVE EmployeeHierarchy AS (
 SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, reports_to
 FROM employee
 -- Filter for North America region
 UNION ALL
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.reports_to
 FROM employee e
 INNER JOIN EmployeeHierarchy eh ON e.reports_to = eh.employee_id
),
ManagerReports AS (
 SELECT eh.reports_to AS manager_id, c.customer_id
 FROM EmployeeHierarchy eh
 JOIN customer c ON eh.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 -- Filter for North America region
)
SELECT m.employee_id AS manager_id,
 m.first_name || ' ' || m.last_name AS manager_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT mr.customer_id) AS total_customers_supported
FROM employee m
LEFT JOIN ManagerReports mr ON m.employee_id = mr.manager_id
 -- Filter for North America region
GROUP BY m.employee_id, m.first_name, m.last_name
ORDER BY total_customers_supported DESC;

question: For each month in the last year, which billing state recorded the highest total invoice amount?
gold_sql:
WITH monthly_state_totals AS (
  SELECT
    DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month,
    billing_state,
    SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
  FROM invoice
  WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
  GROUP BY month, billing_state
), ranked_totals AS (
  SELECT
    month,
    billing_state,
    total_invoice_amount,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY month ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC) AS rank
  FROM monthly_state_totals
)
SELECT
  month,
  billing_state,
  total_invoice_amount
FROM ranked_totals
WHERE rank = 1
ORDER BY month;

question: Which playlists contain tracks composed by more than one composer?

question: Which playlists have the highest total sales from invoice lines and what is the track count in those playlists?

question: For each genre, which album contains the track with the highest unit price?

question: Which genres contain tracks that have durations more than twice the average track length for their genre?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreAvgDuration AS (
  SELECT genre_id, AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_duration
  FROM track
  GROUP BY genre_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT g.name AS genre_name
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN GenreAvgDuration gad ON t.genre_id = gad.genre_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > 2 * gad.avg_duration;

question: Which albums contain tracks that belong to more than two genres, and how many such genres are represented in each album?

JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id

HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 2;

question: Identify genres where the average track price exceeds the overall average track price by at least 20%.
gold_sql:
WITH overall_avg AS (
  SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS avg_price
  FROM track
), genre_avg AS (
  SELECT genre_id, AVG(unit_price) AS avg_genre_price
  FROM track
  GROUP BY genre_id
)
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, ga.avg_genre_price
FROM genre_avg ga
JOIN overall_avg oa ON 1=1
JOIN genre g ON ga.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE ga.avg_genre_price >= 1.2 * oa.avg_price;

question: Can you list the playlists sorted by the average track duration and total number of distinct composers?

       COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) AS total_distinct_composers
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY avg_track_duration_seconds DESC, total_distinct_composers DESC;

question: Which genres have above-average track counts?

            FROM track t
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
            HAVING COUNT(*) > (
                SELECT AVG(cnt) FROM (
                    SELECT COUNT(*) as cnt
                    FROM track
                    GROUP BY genre_id
                ) sub
            )
            ORDER BY track_count DESC

question: Which media types have the highest average unit price for tracks, and what genres are most common in those media types?
gold_sql:
WITH AvgPriceByMediaType AS (
  SELECT
    mt.media_type_id,
    mt.name AS media_type_name,
    AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
  FROM media_type mt
  JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
  GROUP BY mt.media_type_id, mt.name
),
MaxAvgPrice AS (
  SELECT MAX(avg_unit_price) AS max_avg_price FROM AvgPriceByMediaType
),
TopMediaTypes AS (
  SELECT media_type_id, media_type_name, avg_unit_price
  FROM AvgPriceByMediaType
  WHERE avg_unit_price = (SELECT max_avg_price FROM MaxAvgPrice)
),
GenreCounts AS (
  SELECT
    t.media_type_id,
    t.genre_id,
    COUNT(*) AS genre_count
  FROM track t
  WHERE t.media_type_id IN (SELECT media_type_id FROM TopMediaTypes)
  GROUP BY t.media_type_id, t.genre_id
),
GenreNames AS (
  SELECT gc.media_type_id, g.name AS genre_name, gc.genre_count
  FROM GenreCounts gc
  JOIN genre g ON gc.genre_id = g.genre_id
),
MaxGenreCount AS (
  SELECT media_type_id, MAX(genre_count) AS max_genre_count
  FROM GenreCounts
  GROUP BY media_type_id
),
TopGenres AS (
  SELECT gn.media_type_id, gn.genre_name, gn.genre_count
  FROM GenreNames gn
  JOIN MaxGenreCount mgc ON gn.media_type_id = mgc.media_type_id AND gn.genre_count = mgc.max_genre_count
)
SELECT
  t.media_type_name,
  t.avg_unit_price,
  tg.genre_name,
  tg.genre_count
FROM TopMediaTypes t
JOIN TopGenres tg ON t.media_type_id = tg.media_type_id
ORDER BY t.media_type_name, tg.genre_name;

question: For each playlist, what is the distribution of tracks by genre?

SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(t.track_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY p.playlist_id) AS track_percentage
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id

question: Who are the top 5 employees with the highest total sales from their assigned customers?

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: Find the top 3 customers in terms of total spending within each country.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerSpending AS (
    SELECT
        c.customer_id,
        c.first_name,
        c.last_name,
        c.country,
        SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
    FROM customer c
    JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
    WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')  -- North America countries
    GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country
), RankedSpending AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        first_name,
        last_name,
        country,
        total_spending,
        RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY total_spending DESC) AS rank
    FROM CustomerSpending
)
SELECT
    country,
    customer_id,
    first_name,
    last_name,
    total_spending
FROM RankedSpending
WHERE rank <= 3
ORDER BY country, total_spending DESC;

question: Can you identify the playlists that contain tracks from more than five different genres?

JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id

HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 5;

question: Which artist has the longest average track duration across all their albums?

FROM artist ar

JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name

question: How does the total revenue from customers in Europe compare to those in North America over the last year?
gold_sql:
WITH revenue_by_region AS (
  SELECT
    CASE
      WHEN c.country IN ('France', 'Germany', 'United Kingdom', 'Italy', 'Spain', 'Netherlands', 'Belgium', 'Sweden', 'Norway', 'Denmark', 'Finland', 'Ireland', 'Portugal', 'Switzerland', 'Austria', 'Greece', 'Poland', 'Czech Republic', 'Hungary', 'Romania', 'Bulgaria', 'Croatia', 'Slovakia', 'Slovenia', 'Estonia', 'Latvia', 'Lithuania') THEN 'Europe'
      WHEN c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico') THEN 'North America'
      ELSE 'Other'
    END AS region,
    SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
  FROM invoice i
  JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
  WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
    AND c.country IN ('France', 'Germany', 'United Kingdom', 'Italy', 'Spain', 'Netherlands', 'Belgium', 'Sweden', 'Norway', 'Denmark', 'Finland', 'Ireland', 'Portugal', 'Switzerland', 'Austria', 'Greece', 'Poland', 'Czech Republic', 'Hungary', 'Romania', 'Bulgaria', 'Croatia', 'Slovakia', 'Slovenia', 'Estonia', 'Latvia', 'Lithuania', 'USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
  GROUP BY region
)
SELECT
  region,
  total_revenue,
  total_revenue / SUM(total_revenue) OVER () AS revenue_share
FROM revenue_by_region
WHERE region IN ('Europe', 'North America');

question: What is the count of unique tracks appearing across all playlists?

question: Find artists who have tracks in more than 3 different genres

            FROM artist ar
            JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
            JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
            HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.genre_id) > 3
            ORDER BY genre_count DESC

question: Which billing states have the highest total invoice amounts?

FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Which customers have increased their monthly spending by at least 20% compared to the previous month in the past year?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlySpending AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month,
    SUM(total) AS monthly_total
  FROM invoice
  WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
  GROUP BY customer_id, month
),
MonthlySpendingWithPrev AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    month,
    monthly_total,
    LAG(monthly_total) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_total
  FROM MonthlySpending
)
SELECT DISTINCT
  c.customer_id,
  c.first_name,
  c.last_name,
  (ms.monthly_total - ms.prev_month_total) / ms.prev_month_total AS increase_percentage
FROM MonthlySpendingWithPrev ms
JOIN customer c ON ms.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE ms.prev_month_total IS NOT NULL
  AND (ms.monthly_total - ms.prev_month_total) / ms.prev_month_total >= 0.20
ORDER BY increase_percentage DESC;

question: Which albums have generated the highest total sales in the last six months?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY total_sales DESC;

question: How many albums include tracks from the Rock genre, and who are the corresponding artists?

question: Can you find playlists that contain tracks from more than three different genres?

question: Which countries have the highest total invoice amounts?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: What is the total sales amount by media type for invoices billed in Canada?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM invoice i
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
WHERE i.billing_country = 'Canada'
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY total_sales DESC;

question: For each genre, what is the proportion of tracks appearing in playlists compared to the total tracks in that genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT t.track_id) AS total_tracks,
       COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) AS tracks_in_playlists,
       CAST(COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(DISTINCT t.track_id) AS proportion_in_playlists
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
LEFT JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: What is the total number of tracks and total revenue for each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
 COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: How many invoices were issued in the year 2023?
Identify the top 5 customers by total spending who purchased tracks from more than one genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
ORDER BY total_spending DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Which customers have spent more than the average spending of all customers and purchased tracks from at least five distinct genres?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerSpending AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.first_name,
    c.last_name,
    SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spent
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
),
AverageSpending AS (
  SELECT AVG(total_spent) AS avg_spent FROM CustomerSpending
),
CustomerGenres AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genres
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
  JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY c.customer_id
)
SELECT
  cs.customer_id,
  cs.first_name,
  cs.last_name,
  cs.total_spent,
  cg.distinct_genres
FROM CustomerSpending cs
JOIN AverageSpending a ON cs.total_spent > a.avg_spent
JOIN CustomerGenres cg ON cs.customer_id = cg.customer_id
WHERE cg.distinct_genres >= 5
ORDER BY cs.total_spent DESC;

question: What is the average invoice total for customers grouped by support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: What is the difference in average track duration between albums released by the top 5 artists with the most albums?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistAlbumCount AS (
  SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
  FROM album
  GROUP BY artist_id
  ORDER BY album_count DESC
  LIMIT 5
),
TopArtistsTracks AS (
  SELECT a.artist_id, t.track_id, t.milliseconds
  FROM artist a
  JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  WHERE a.artist_id IN (SELECT artist_id FROM ArtistAlbumCount)
),
ArtistAvgTrackDuration AS (
  SELECT artist_id, AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration
  FROM TopArtistsTracks
  GROUP BY artist_id
)
SELECT 
  MAX(avg_track_duration) - MIN(avg_track_duration) AS difference_in_avg_track_duration
FROM ArtistAvgTrackDuration;

question: Which playlists have at least one track from every genre found in the playlist’s tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) = (
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id)
    FROM track
    WHERE track_id IN (
        SELECT track_id
        FROM playlist_track
        WHERE playlist_id = p.playlist_id
    )
)

question: Identify employees who do not manage anyone and have more than 10 customers assigned to them.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee m ON e.employee_id = m.reports_to
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
WHERE m.employee_id IS NULL
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(c.customer_id) > 10;

question: Among employees, who supports customers with the highest average number of invoices per year?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) * 1.0 / (MAX(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM i.invoice_date)) - MIN(EXTRACT(YEAR FROM i.invoice_date)) + 1) AS avg_invoices_per_year
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoices_per_year DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which albums contain tracks composed by more than one composer?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title
FROM album a
JOIN (
    SELECT album_id
    FROM track
    WHERE composer IS NOT NULL AND composer <> ''
    GROUP BY album_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT composer) > 1
) t ON a.album_id = t.album_id;

question: Determine the employee with the largest difference in invoice totals among their supported customers.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerInvoiceTotals AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 c.customer_id,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id
),
EmployeeInvoiceDiff AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 MAX(total_invoice_amount) - MIN(total_invoice_amount) AS invoice_total_diff
 FROM CustomerInvoiceTotals
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 eid.invoice_total_diff
FROM EmployeeInvoiceDiff eid
JOIN employee e ON eid.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
ORDER BY eid.invoice_total_diff DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Can you provide the count of customers by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT country, COUNT(customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM customer
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY customer_count DESC;

question: Which playlists contain tracks that appear in invoices with the highest total sales?
gold_sql:
WITH InvoiceSales AS (
  SELECT invoice_id, total
  FROM invoice
),
TrackSales AS (
  SELECT il.track_id, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
  FROM invoice_line il
  JOIN InvoiceSales i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
  GROUP BY il.track_id
),
MaxSales AS (
  SELECT MAX(total_sales) AS max_total_sales
  FROM TrackSales
),
TracksWithMaxSales AS (
  SELECT ts.track_id
  FROM TrackSales ts
  JOIN MaxSales ms ON ts.total_sales = ms.max_total_sales
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN TracksWithMaxSales twms ON pt.track_id = twms.track_id;

question: Find playlists that have at least one track from every media type available.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) = (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM media_type);

question: What is the total revenue for 2022?

SELECT SUM(total) FROM invoice
            WHERE invoice_date >= '2022-01-01' AND invoice_date < '2023-01-01'

question: How many unique cities do our customers come from?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT city) FROM customer

question: How many tracks are in each genre?

SELECT g.name, COUNT(*) as track_count
            FROM track t
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name

question: How do invoice totals vary by country and by month?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  billing_country AS country,
  TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY billing_country, year_month
ORDER BY billing_country, year_month;

question: Show each invoice with a running total of revenue
gold_sql:
SELECT
                invoice_id,
                invoice_date,
                total,
                SUM(total) OVER (ORDER BY invoice_date, invoice_id) as running_total
            FROM invoice
            ORDER BY invoice_date, invoice_id

question: Find the albums with the largest number of tracks that appear in at least two different playlists.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, COUNT(t.track_id) AS num_tracks
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
WHERE t.track_id IN (
    SELECT pt.track_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    GROUP BY pt.track_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) >= 2
)
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY num_tracks DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: List employees along with the count of customers they support and the total invoice amount generated by those customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count, COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: List all employees hired before 2003

            FROM employee

question: What is the total number of tracks available for each artist across all their albums?

JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
