question: How does the average invoice total vary between customers in different states?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.state, AVG(i.total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.state
ORDER BY c.state;

question: How many tracks have a composer specified?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS tracks_with_composer FROM track WHERE composer IS NOT NULL AND composer <> ''

question: Identify the employees who support customers with the lowest average invoice totals.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerAvgInvoice AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.support_rep_id,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.support_rep_id
),
MinAvgInvoice AS (
 SELECT MIN(avg_invoice_total) AS min_avg_total
 FROM CustomerAvgInvoice
)

SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 e.title,
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name AS customer_first_name,
 c.last_name AS customer_last_name,
 cai.avg_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN CustomerAvgInvoice cai ON e.employee_id = cai.support_rep_id
JOIN customer c ON cai.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN MinAvgInvoice m ON cai.avg_invoice_total = m.min_avg_total
 ORDER BY e.employee_id, c.customer_id;

question: For each artist, what is the longest track duration and which track is it?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name, t.track_id, t.name AS track_name, t.milliseconds
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
WHERE t.milliseconds = (
    SELECT MAX(t2.milliseconds)
    FROM track t2
    JOIN album al2 ON t2.album_id = al2.album_id
    WHERE al2.artist_id = a.artist_id
)
ORDER BY a.artist_id;

question: What is the total number of employees who live in the city of Toronto?
What is the distribution of track counts per media type within each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, mt.name AS media_type_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(t.track_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY g.genre_id) AS track_percentage
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name, mt.media_type_id, mt.name
ORDER BY g.name, mt.name;

question: List employees by the total invoice amounts of their supported customers, ordered from highest to lowest.
gold_sql:

FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Which customers purchased the most tracks in terms of quantity?

 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name

LIMIT 10;

question: What is the total number of albums released for each genre?

JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id

question: Which customers have purchased the most distinct tracks and what is their total spending?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT il.track_id) AS distinct_tracks_purchased, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name

question: What is the average and maximum invoice total per billing country for invoices issued in the past year?

FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY billing_country;

question: What is the average tenure of employees in years?

SELECT AVG(EXTRACT(year FROM AGE(CURRENT_DATE, hire_date))) AS average_tenure_years
FROM employee

question: Which genre has the fewest number of tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM genre g
LEFT JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY track_count ASC
LIMIT 1;

question: For each employee, what is the average total invoice amount of their customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: For each playlist, show the most common composer and the count of their tracks in that playlist.
gold_sql:
WITH ComposerCounts AS (
    SELECT
        pt.playlist_id,
        t.composer,
        COUNT(*) AS track_count
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    WHERE t.composer IS NOT NULL AND t.composer <> ''
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, t.composer
),
MaxCounts AS (
    SELECT
        playlist_id,
        MAX(track_count) AS max_count
    FROM ComposerCounts
    GROUP BY playlist_id
)
SELECT
    p.playlist_id,
    p.name AS playlist_name,
    cc.composer AS most_common_composer,
    cc.track_count
FROM MaxCounts mc
JOIN ComposerCounts cc ON mc.playlist_id = cc.playlist_id AND mc.max_count = cc.track_count
JOIN playlist p ON p.playlist_id = mc.playlist_id
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: How many unique customers have been assigned to each support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS unique_customers
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY unique_customers DESC;

question: Which employees manage customers who have purchased the most diverse genres of music?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerGenreCount AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id AS employee_id,
 c.customer_id,
 COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genres
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id
),
EmployeeMaxGenreDiversity AS (
 SELECT
 employee_id,
 MAX(distinct_genres) AS max_distinct_genres
 FROM CustomerGenreCount
 GROUP BY employee_id
),
MaxDiversity AS (
 SELECT MAX(max_distinct_genres) AS max_diversity FROM EmployeeMaxGenreDiversity
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 emgd.max_distinct_genres
FROM EmployeeMaxGenreDiversity emgd
JOIN MaxDiversity md ON emgd.max_distinct_genres = md.max_diversity
JOIN employee e ON emgd.employee_id = e.employee_id
 ORDER BY emgd.max_distinct_genres DESC;

question: How many invoices were created by customers living in California last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.state = 'CA'
  AND i.invoice_date >= DATE('2023-01-01')
  AND i.invoice_date < DATE('2024-01-01');

question: Which employees have the highest number of customers assigned as support representatives?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY customer_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which employees have customers that purchased tracks from the greatest number of different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genres
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) = (
 SELECT MAX(genre_count) FROM (
 SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT t2.genre_id) AS genre_count
 FROM employee e2
 JOIN customer c2 ON e2.employee_id = c2.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i2 ON c2.customer_id = i2.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il2 ON i2.invoice_id = il2.invoice_id
 JOIN track t2 ON il2.track_id = t2.track_id
 GROUP BY e2.employee_id
 ) sub
)
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: How does the number of customers per support representative correlate with the total invoice amount those customers generate?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS num_customers, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id
ORDER BY num_customers DESC;

question: Which customers have spent more than the average invoice total for invoices issued during their first year as customers?
gold_sql:
WITH FirstYearInvoices AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 i.invoice_id,
 i.invoice_date,
 i.total,
 MIN(i.invoice_date) OVER (PARTITION BY c.customer_id) AS first_invoice_date
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 ),

CustomerFirstYearTotals AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 SUM(total) AS total_spent_first_year
 FROM FirstYearInvoices
 WHERE invoice_date <= first_invoice_date + INTERVAL '1 year'
 GROUP BY customer_id
),

AverageFirstYearInvoiceTotal AS (
 SELECT AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_total
 FROM FirstYearInvoices
 WHERE invoice_date <= first_invoice_date + INTERVAL '1 year'
)

SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cft.total_spent_first_year
FROM customer c
JOIN CustomerFirstYearTotals cft ON c.customer_id = cft.customer_id
JOIN AverageFirstYearInvoiceTotal afy ON 1=1
WHERE cft.total_spent_first_year > afy.avg_invoice_total
 ORDER BY c.customer_id;

question: Which customers have purchased tracks from a specific media type and what is their total spending?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, mt.name AS media_type_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, mt.name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: Which playlists feature tracks from at least 5 different artists and how many tracks do they contain?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.name AS playlist_name,
       COUNT(pt.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) >= 5;

question: Identify genres where the longest track exceeds twice the average track length for that genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
HAVING MAX(t.milliseconds) > 2 * AVG(t.milliseconds);

question: Which managers have the highest average customer invoice totals based on the support representatives reporting to them?
gold_sql:
SELECT m.employee_id AS manager_id, m.first_name || ' ' || m.last_name AS manager_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee m
JOIN employee e ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
JOIN customer c ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY m.employee_id, m.first_name, m.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: What is the total quantity of tracks sold according to invoice lines?
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity_sold FROM invoice_line;

question: What is the total spending for customers in each city?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.city, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.city
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: How does the average invoice total vary among customers from different cities?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.city, AVG(i.total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.city
ORDER BY average_invoice_total DESC;

question: Which customers have not made any purchases for at least six consecutive months within the last two years?
gold_sql:
WITH months AS (
 SELECT DISTINCT
 DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month_start
 FROM invoice
 WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 years'
),
customer_months AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 m.month_start,
 CASE WHEN i.invoice_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS made_purchase
 FROM customer c
 CROSS JOIN months m
 LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 AND DATE_TRUNC('month', i.invoice_date) = m.month_start
 ),
consecutive_no_purchase AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 month_start,
 made_purchase,
 ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month_start) -
 ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id, made_purchase ORDER BY month_start) AS grp
 FROM customer_months
 WHERE made_purchase = 0
),
six_month_gaps AS (
 SELECT
 customer_id,
 COUNT(*) AS no_purchase_months
 FROM consecutive_no_purchase
 GROUP BY customer_id, grp
 HAVING COUNT(*) >= 6
)
SELECT DISTINCT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name
FROM six_month_gaps s
JOIN customer c ON s.customer_id = c.customer_id
 ORDER BY c.customer_id;

question: Which artists have the highest total sales revenue?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) as revenue
            FROM invoice_line il
            JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
            JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
            JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
            GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
            ORDER BY revenue DESC
            LIMIT 10

question: How does the total spending of customers vary across different billing cities?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_spending
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY total_spending DESC;

question: For each artist, what is the total size in bytes of all their tracks, and which artists are in the top five by this metric?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_bytes DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: Which artists have albums featuring tracks in the 'Rock' genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name = 'Rock'
GROUP BY a.name;

question: Which playlists include tracks that have appeared in the most other playlists?
gold_sql:
WITH TrackPlaylistCounts AS (
    SELECT
        track_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT playlist_id) AS playlist_count
    FROM playlist_track
    GROUP BY track_id
),
MaxPlaylistCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(playlist_count) AS max_count FROM TrackPlaylistCounts
),
TracksWithMaxCount AS (
    SELECT track_id
    FROM TrackPlaylistCounts, MaxPlaylistCount
    WHERE playlist_count = max_count
),
PlaylistsWithMaxTracks AS (
    SELECT DISTINCT pt.playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN TracksWithMaxCount tmax ON pt.track_id = tmax.track_id
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN PlaylistsWithMaxTracks pwm ON p.playlist_id = pwm.playlist_id
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: Which genres have the longest average track duration, and how do they rank compared to each other?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_duration_seconds
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_duration_seconds DESC;

question: What are the top 5 playlists that contain tracks with the longest average duration?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY avg_duration DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the total number of invoices generated per year?
gold_sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) AS year, COUNT(invoice_id) AS total_invoices
FROM invoice
GROUP BY year
ORDER BY year;

question: How many distinct composers are in the track database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT composer) FROM track WHERE composer IS NOT NULL

question: What is the average number of invoices per customer for those supported by each employee?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  e.employee_id,
  e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS employee_name,
  AVG(COALESCE(invoice_count, 0)) AS avg_invoices_per_customer
FROM
  employee e
JOIN
  customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN
  (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count FROM invoice GROUP BY customer_id) i
ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY
  e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY
  e.employee_id;

question: Find artists whose average track duration in their albums is greater than the overall artist average duration.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
HAVING AVG(t.milliseconds) > (
    SELECT AVG(t3.milliseconds)
    FROM artist a3
    JOIN album al3 ON a3.artist_id = al3.artist_id
    JOIN track t3 ON al3.album_id = t3.album_id
)

question: How many tracks from each media type are included in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, mt.media_type_id, mt.name AS media_type_name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, mt.media_type_id, mt.name
ORDER BY pt.playlist_id, mt.media_type_id;

question: What is the average number of invoices per billing city?
What is the average invoice total for customers grouped by their assigned support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: What is the average invoice total by billing state for customers in the United States?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM invoice
WHERE billing_country = 'USA'
GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY billing_state;

question: Which genres have the highest average track price and how do they compare?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC;

question: List the total spending grouped by customer city and country.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.city, c.country, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.city, c.country
ORDER BY c.country, c.city;

question: Which artists have albums where no track exceeds the average track unit price for that artist's albums?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistAvgPrice AS (
    SELECT ar.artist_id, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
    FROM artist ar
    JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
    JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY ar.artist_id
),
AlbumMaxTrackPrice AS (
    SELECT al.album_id, al.artist_id, MAX(t.unit_price) AS max_track_price
    FROM album al
    JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY al.album_id, al.artist_id
)
SELECT DISTINCT ar.artist_id, ar.name
FROM artist ar
JOIN AlbumMaxTrackPrice amp ON ar.artist_id = amp.artist_id
JOIN ArtistAvgPrice aap ON ar.artist_id = aap.artist_id
WHERE amp.max_track_price <= aap.avg_unit_price
ORDER BY ar.name;

question: Which media types have the longest average track duration and how many tracks belong to each type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, 
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_duration_seconds, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM media_type mt
JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.media_type_id, mt.name
ORDER BY avg_duration_seconds DESC;

question: Can you provide a list of albums along with their artists and the total number of tracks in each album?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.title AS album_title, ar.name AS artist_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM album a
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
LEFT JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title, ar.name
ORDER BY a.title;

question: Which employees support customers with the highest average spending on jazz genre tracks?
gold_sql:
WITH JazzGenre AS (
 SELECT genre_id
 FROM genre
 WHERE name = 'Jazz'
),
JazzTrackSpending AS (
 SELECT i.customer_id, il.invoice_line_id, il.unit_price * il.quantity AS spending
 FROM invoice_line il
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN JazzGenre jg ON t.genre_id = jg.genre_id
 JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
),
CustomerJazzSpending AS (
 SELECT customer_id, AVG(spending) AS avg_jazz_spending
 FROM JazzTrackSpending
 GROUP BY customer_id
),
EmployeeCustomerSpending AS (
 SELECT c.support_rep_id AS employee_id, AVG(cjs.avg_jazz_spending) AS avg_spending_per_employee
 FROM customer c
 JOIN CustomerJazzSpending cjs ON c.customer_id = cjs.customer_id
 -- North America countries
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id
),
MaxAvgSpending AS (
 SELECT MAX(avg_spending_per_employee) AS max_avg_spending
 FROM EmployeeCustomerSpending
)
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, ecs.avg_spending_per_employee
FROM EmployeeCustomerSpending ecs
JOIN MaxAvgSpending mas ON ecs.avg_spending_per_employee = mas.max_avg_spending
JOIN employee e ON ecs.employee_id = e.employee_id;

question: Which customers have spent the most on jazz genre tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spent_on_jazz
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name = 'Jazz'
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country
ORDER BY total_spent_on_jazz DESC;

question: What is the count of playlists grouped by the number of unique media types represented in their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  unique_media_type_count,
  COUNT(playlist_id) AS playlist_count
FROM (
  SELECT
    pt.playlist_id,
    COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) AS unique_media_type_count
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
) AS media_type_counts
GROUP BY unique_media_type_count
ORDER BY unique_media_type_count;

question: Which cities have the highest number of invoices with total amounts above the overall average?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, COUNT(*) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
WHERE total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM invoice)
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: Identify playlists where all tracks belong to the same genre and calculate the total track count for each.
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) = 1;

question: What is the total number of tracks per genre and their average unit price, sorted by highest average price?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_unit_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY average_unit_price DESC;

question: Which media types have the highest average invoice line unit price for tracks sold?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, AVG(il.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY avg_unit_price DESC;

question: Find the customers whose total spending is below the median spending of all customers.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerSpending AS (
  SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS total_spending
  FROM invoice
  GROUP BY customer_id
),
MedianSpending AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_spending) AS median_spending
  FROM CustomerSpending
)
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, cs.total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN CustomerSpending cs ON c.customer_id = cs.customer_id
JOIN MedianSpending ms ON 1=1
WHERE cs.total_spending < ms.median_spending
ORDER BY cs.total_spending DESC;

question: How many tracks belong to each media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.media_type_id, mt.name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM media_type mt
LEFT JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.media_type_id, mt.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC;

question: What is the average number of employees managed by each manager?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(employee_count) AS avg_employees_per_manager
FROM (
 SELECT reports_to AS manager_id, COUNT(employee_id) AS employee_count
 FROM employee
 GROUP BY reports_to
 HAVING reports_to IS NOT NULL
) AS manager_employee_counts;

question: How many playlists have more than 100 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_more_than_100_tracks
FROM (
    SELECT playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track
    GROUP BY playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(track_id) > 100
) AS subquery;

question: Find the employees with the fewest customers but the highest total invoice amounts from those customers.
gold_sql:
WITH EmployeeCustomerStats AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
),
MinCustomerCount AS (
 SELECT MIN(customer_count) AS min_customer_count
 FROM EmployeeCustomerStats
),
MaxInvoiceAmount AS (
 SELECT MAX(total_invoice_amount) AS max_invoice_amount
 FROM EmployeeCustomerStats
 WHERE customer_count = (SELECT min_customer_count FROM MinCustomerCount)
)
SELECT
 ecs.employee_id,
 ecs.first_name,
 ecs.last_name,
 ecs.customer_count,
 ecs.total_invoice_amount
FROM EmployeeCustomerStats ecs
JOIN MinCustomerCount mcc ON ecs.customer_count = mcc.min_customer_count
JOIN MaxInvoiceAmount mia ON ecs.total_invoice_amount = mia.max_invoice_amount;

question: How many albums does each artist have?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name, COUNT(*) as album_count
            FROM album al
            JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
            GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
            ORDER BY album_count DESC

question: Identify albums with the largest total file size of tracks and the artists associated with those albums.
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumFileSize AS (
  SELECT
    a.album_id,
    a.title AS album_title,
    ar.name AS artist_name,
    SUM(t.bytes) AS total_file_size
  FROM album a
  JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
  JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
  GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title, ar.name
)
SELECT album_title, artist_name, total_file_size
FROM AlbumFileSize
WHERE total_file_size = (
  SELECT MAX(total_file_size) FROM AlbumFileSize
);

question: Which billing states have the highest average invoice amounts?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Find employees and the number of customers assigned to them, ranked by the total amount of invoices generated by their customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS num_customers, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Which employees have customers that frequently purchase tracks in the same genre as the employee's favorite genre?
gold_sql:
WITH EmployeeFavoriteGenre AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 t.genre_id,
 g.name AS genre_name,
 COUNT(*) AS genre_track_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, t.genre_id, g.name
),
EmployeeTopGenre AS (
 SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, genre_id, genre_name
 FROM (
 SELECT *, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY employee_id ORDER BY genre_track_count DESC) AS rank
 FROM EmployeeFavoriteGenre
 ) ranked
 WHERE rank = 1
),
CustomerGenrePurchases AS (
 SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 t.genre_id,
 COUNT(*) AS purchase_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, t.genre_id
),
FrequentCustomerPurchases AS (
 SELECT
 cgp.employee_id,
 cgp.genre_id
 FROM CustomerGenrePurchases cgp
 JOIN EmployeeTopGenre etg ON cgp.employee_id = etg.employee_id AND cgp.genre_id = etg.genre_id
 WHERE cgp.purchase_count > 5
)
SELECT DISTINCT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name
FROM employee e
JOIN FrequentCustomerPurchases fcp ON e.employee_id = fcp.employee_id
 ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which genres have more than 100 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name, COUNT(*) as track_count
            FROM track t
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 100
            ORDER BY track_count DESC

question: List albums whose tracks have a median price higher than the overall median track price.
gold_sql:
WITH OverallMedian AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS median_price
  FROM track
),
AlbumMedian AS (
  SELECT album_id, PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS median_price
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
)
SELECT a.album_id, a.title
FROM album a
JOIN AlbumMedian am ON a.album_id = am.album_id
JOIN OverallMedian om ON 1=1
WHERE am.median_price > om.median_price;

question: How many tracks exist in each genre and what is their average unit price?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_unit_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which customer has made the highest total purchases in the last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_purchases
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_purchases DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Identify the albums that contain tracks from more than two different genres.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title
FROM album a
JOIN (
    SELECT album_id
    FROM track
    GROUP BY album_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id) > 2
) t ON a.album_id = t.album_id;

question: How many albums are there for each artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name AS artist_name, COUNT(al.album_id) AS album_count
FROM artist ar
LEFT JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY album_count DESC;

question: Among all employees, who manages customers generating the highest median invoice total per month?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlyInvoiceTotals AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 i.customer_id,
 DATE_TRUNC('month', i.invoice_date) AS invoice_month,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_per_month
 FROM invoice i
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, i.customer_id, invoice_month
),
MedianInvoiceTotals AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 customer_id,
 PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY total_per_month) AS median_monthly_invoice
 FROM MonthlyInvoiceTotals
 GROUP BY support_rep_id, customer_id
),
SupportRepMedian AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY median_monthly_invoice) AS median_invoice_per_month
 FROM MedianInvoiceTotals
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id AS support_rep_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 srm.median_invoice_per_month
FROM SupportRepMedian srm
JOIN employee e ON srm.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
ORDER BY srm.median_invoice_per_month DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average track duration grouped by genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration_milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which artists have albums containing tracks from the greatest number of different genres?
gold_sql:
WITH artist_genre_counts AS (
  SELECT artist.artist_id, artist.name, COUNT(DISTINCT track.genre_id) AS genre_count
  FROM artist
  JOIN album ON artist.artist_id = album.artist_id
  JOIN track ON album.album_id = track.album_id
  GROUP BY artist.artist_id, artist.name
)
SELECT name, genre_count
FROM artist_genre_counts
WHERE genre_count = (SELECT MAX(genre_count) FROM artist_genre_counts);

question: List the playlists that exclusively contain tracks from a single genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) = 1;

question: How many customers have billing addresses in California?
Identify employees who support customers with the largest total invoice amounts and the count of those customers.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerTotalInvoice AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 c.customer_id,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id
),
SupportRepTotal AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 SUM(total_invoice_amount) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM CustomerTotalInvoice
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
),
MaxSupportRepTotal AS (
 SELECT MAX(total_invoice_amount) AS max_total_invoice_amount
 FROM SupportRepTotal
)
SELECT
 e.employee_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name,
 srt.total_invoice_amount AS total_invoice_amount,
 COUNT(cti.customer_id) AS customer_count
FROM SupportRepTotal srt
JOIN MaxSupportRepTotal msrt ON srt.total_invoice_amount = msrt.max_total_invoice_amount
JOIN employee e ON srt.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN CustomerTotalInvoice cti ON e.employee_id = cti.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, srt.total_invoice_amount
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: What is the average number of invoices per customer?
Can you show the average sales revenue generated by customers assigned to each support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_sales_revenue
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_sales_revenue DESC;

question: What is the average unit price of tracks in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pl.playlist_id, pl.name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_unit_price
FROM playlist pl
JOIN playlist_track pt ON pl.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY pl.playlist_id, pl.name
ORDER BY pl.playlist_id;

question: Which employees have been with the company the longest and how many customers do they currently support?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.hire_date, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customers_supported
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.hire_date
ORDER BY e.hire_date ASC
LIMIT 5;

question: Can you list the top 10 longest tracks and the albums and artists they belong to?
gold_sql:
SELECT t.name AS track_name, a.title AS album_title, ar.name AS artist_name, t.milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
ORDER BY t.milliseconds DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the average number of tracks per album for each artist, and which artists exceed this average?
gold_sql:
WITH artist_album_track_counts AS (
  SELECT
    a.artist_id,
    a.name AS artist_name,
    al.album_id,
    COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
  FROM artist a
  JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
  LEFT JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name, al.album_id
),
artist_avg_tracks AS (
  SELECT
    artist_id,
    artist_name,
    AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_album
  FROM artist_album_track_counts
  GROUP BY artist_id, artist_name
)
SELECT
  aatc.artist_id,
  aatc.artist_name,
  aatc.album_id,
  aatc.track_count,
  aat.avg_tracks_per_album
FROM artist_album_track_counts aatc
JOIN artist_avg_tracks aat ON aatc.artist_id = aat.artist_id
WHERE aatc.track_count > aat.avg_tracks_per_album
ORDER BY aatc.artist_name, aatc.album_id;

question: List the employees and the names of their managers along with the total number of customers they individually support.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name AS employee_first_name, e.last_name AS employee_last_name,
 m.first_name AS manager_first_name, m.last_name AS manager_last_name,
 COUNT(c.customer_id) AS total_customers
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, m.first_name, m.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: What is the average invoice total by customer country, for countries with more than 5 customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, AVG(i.total) as avg_invoice
            FROM invoice i
            JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
            WHERE c.country IN (
                SELECT country FROM customer
                GROUP BY country
                HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
            )
            GROUP BY c.country
            ORDER BY avg_invoice DESC

question: What is the sum of invoice totals for customers in the United States?
gold_sql:
SELECT SUM(i.total) AS sum_invoice_totals_us
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE c.country = 'USA' OR c.country = 'United States' OR c.country = 'United States of America';

question: Among all playlists, which one contains the highest total duration of tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, SUM(t.milliseconds) AS total_duration_milliseconds
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_duration_milliseconds DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total quantity of tracks sold for each media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold
FROM media_type mt
JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY total_quantity_sold DESC;

question: Find support representatives whose customers have the highest average number of tracks purchased per invoice.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_invoice
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN (
 SELECT invoice_id, COUNT(*) AS track_count
 FROM invoice_line
 GROUP BY invoice_id
) il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
WHERE e.title LIKE '%Support%'
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_tracks_per_invoice DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average unit price and total revenue of tracks grouped by playlists with more than 50 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name,
       AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_unit_price,
       SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) > 50;

question: Which playlists feature tracks by artists who have albums in more than one genre?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistGenres AS (
  SELECT a.artist_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
  FROM artist a
  JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  GROUP BY a.artist_id
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN ArtistGenres ag ON al.artist_id = ag.artist_id
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: How many invoices were issued each month in the past year, grouped by billing country?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  to_char(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  billing_country,
  COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= current_date - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY year_month, billing_country
ORDER BY year_month, billing_country;

question: Which genres have the longest average track duration, and which have the shortest?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT 
        g.genre_id,
        g.name AS genre_name,
        AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
    FROM genre g
    JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
    GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
)
SELECT 
    genre_name,
    avg_duration
FROM GenreAvgDuration
WHERE avg_duration = (SELECT MAX(avg_duration) FROM GenreAvgDuration)
   OR avg_duration = (SELECT MIN(avg_duration) FROM GenreAvgDuration);

question: Which playlists feature the most expensive tracks on average?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY avg_unit_price DESC;

question: Identify the top 5 customers by total spending and the number of distinct genres they purchased tracks from.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, 
       SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_spending, 
       COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genres
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the average duration of tracks in the track table?
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(milliseconds) AS average_duration_milliseconds FROM track;

question: What is the total revenue by genre from purchases made by customers in each country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, g.name AS genre, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.country, g.name
ORDER BY c.country, total_revenue DESC;

question: What is the maximum number of tracks in a single album?
What is the distribution of the number of tracks per playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  track_count,
  COUNT(*) AS playlist_count,
  COUNT(*) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM playlist) AS percentage
FROM (
  SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist_track
  GROUP BY playlist_id
) AS track_counts
GROUP BY track_count
ORDER BY track_count;

question: List employees along with the count of customers they support and the total sales those customers have generated.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count,
 COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_sales
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: What is the average unit price of tracks grouped by composer?
gold_sql:
SELECT composer, AVG(unit_price) AS average_unit_price
FROM track
GROUP BY composer
ORDER BY composer;

question: What is the average purchase amount per invoice by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country AS country, AVG(total) AS average_purchase_amount_per_invoice
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY billing_country;

question: Which genres have the highest total number of albums associated with their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) AS total_albums
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY total_albums DESC;

question: Which customers have not made any purchases in the last year but have invoices before that?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
FROM customer c
WHERE c.customer_id IN (
    SELECT i.customer_id
    FROM invoice i
    WHERE i.invoice_date < CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
) AND c.customer_id NOT IN (
    SELECT i.customer_id
    FROM invoice i
    WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
)

question: Which playlists contain tracks that belong to the top 3 most popular genres by total track count in the catalog?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreTrackCounts AS (
    SELECT genre_id, COUNT(*) AS track_count
    FROM track
    GROUP BY genre_id
), TopGenres AS (
    SELECT genre_id
    FROM GenreTrackCounts
    ORDER BY track_count DESC
    LIMIT 3
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.genre_id IN (SELECT genre_id FROM TopGenres);

question: How many invoices were issued in the last month?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 month'
  AND invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE);

question: Which artists have albums that contain tracks of multiple media types?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) > 1;

question: What is the total sales amount by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, SUM(i.total) as total_sales
            FROM invoice i
            JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
            GROUP BY c.country
            ORDER BY total_sales DESC

question: What is the average, minimum, and maximum track duration for each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration,
       MIN(t.milliseconds) AS min_duration,
       MAX(t.milliseconds) AS max_duration
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Identify the customers whose total spending exceeded the average spending of all customers in their country.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerSpending AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.first_name,
    c.last_name,
    c.country,
    SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  WHERE c.country = 'USA' OR c.country = 'Canada' OR c.country = 'Mexico'
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.country
),
CountryAverageSpending AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    AVG(total_spending) AS avg_spending
  FROM CustomerSpending
  GROUP BY country
)
SELECT
  cs.customer_id,
  cs.first_name,
  cs.last_name,
  cs.country,
  cs.total_spending
FROM CustomerSpending cs
JOIN CountryAverageSpending cas ON cs.country = cas.country
WHERE cs.total_spending > cas.avg_spending
ORDER BY cs.country, cs.total_spending DESC;

question: What is the distribution of total invoice amounts across different media types?
gold_sql:
WITH media_totals AS (
 SELECT mt.name AS media_type, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_invoice_amount
 FROM invoice_line il
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
 JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY mt.name
), total_sum AS (
 SELECT SUM(total_invoice_amount) AS grand_total FROM media_totals
)
SELECT media_type, total_invoice_amount, 
 total_invoice_amount / grand_total AS percentage_of_total
FROM media_totals, total_sum
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Who are the managers that have the largest number of direct reports in the company?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee r ON r.reports_to = e.employee_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(r.employee_id) = (
 SELECT MAX(direct_reports_count) FROM (
 SELECT COUNT(r2.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
 FROM employee e2
 LEFT JOIN employee r2 ON r2.reports_to = e2.employee_id
 GROUP BY e2.employee_id
 ) AS counts
)

question: Which customers have purchased tracks in more than five different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 5;

question: What is the count of tracks and total size in bytes for playlists that include tracks from the Rock genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT
    COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) AS track_count,
    SUM(t.bytes) AS total_size_bytes
FROM
    playlist_track pt
JOIN
    track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN
    genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE
    g.name = 'Rock';

question: How many customers does each employee support, and what is the total invoice amount from those customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS number_of_customers, COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which billing states have the highest count of invoices during the past year?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
 GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: What is the average duration of tracks by media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY mt.name;

question: For each employee, what is the average total invoice amount of the customers they support, ranked by highest to lowest?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Which media type has the greatest number of tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count
FROM media_type mt
JOIN track t ON mt.media_type_id = t.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What are the top 3 most common media types for tracks by the artist with the most albums?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistAlbumCounts AS (
  SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
  FROM album
  GROUP BY artist_id
),
TopArtist AS (
  SELECT artist_id
  FROM ArtistAlbumCounts
  ORDER BY album_count DESC
  LIMIT 1
),
TracksByTopArtist AS (
  SELECT t.media_type_id
  FROM track t
  JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
  JOIN TopArtist ta ON a.artist_id = ta.artist_id
),
MediaTypeCounts AS (
  SELECT media_type_id, COUNT(*) AS track_count
  FROM TracksByTopArtist
  GROUP BY media_type_id
  ORDER BY track_count DESC
  LIMIT 3
),
TotalTracks AS (
  SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_count
  FROM TracksByTopArtist
)
SELECT mt.name AS media_type_name,
       mtc.track_count,
       CAST(mtc.track_count AS FLOAT) / tt.total_count AS track_percentage
FROM MediaTypeCounts mtc
JOIN media_type mt ON mtc.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
CROSS JOIN TotalTracks tt
ORDER BY mtc.track_count DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the total number of tracks in playlists that include songs from the Jazz genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT pt.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE pt.playlist_id IN (
    SELECT DISTINCT pt2.playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt2
    JOIN track t2 ON pt2.track_id = t2.track_id
    JOIN genre g2 ON t2.genre_id = g2.genre_id
    WHERE g2.name = 'Jazz'
);

question: How many albums are available in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS total_albums FROM album;

question: Which playlists feature tracks composed by more than one composer?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) > 1;

question: List all employees along with their direct manager's name.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name AS employee_first_name, e.last_name AS employee_last_name, 
       m.first_name AS manager_first_name, m.last_name AS manager_last_name
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id;

question: Identify the top 5 customers with the highest total spending and the number of invoices they have.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_spending, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the distribution of track counts per genre within each album?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  a.album_id,
  a.title AS album_title,
  g.genre_id,
  g.name AS genre_name,
  COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count,
  COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(t.track_id)) OVER (PARTITION BY a.album_id) AS track_count_percentage
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title, g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY a.album_id, track_count DESC;

question: Which employees have the highest number of customers assigned to them?
gold_sql:
WITH EmployeeCustomerCounts AS (
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
)
SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name, customer_count
FROM EmployeeCustomerCounts
WHERE customer_count = (SELECT MAX(customer_count) FROM EmployeeCustomerCounts);

question: Which playlists feature tracks exclusively from a single artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) = 1;

question: Which genres have the highest average track price when considering only tracks longer than five minutes?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE t.milliseconds > 300000  -- 5 minutes in milliseconds
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_price DESC;

question: What is the average unit price of all tracks?
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS average_unit_price FROM track;

question: Which genre has the highest average revenue per track sold?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) / SUM(il.quantity) AS avg_revenue_per_track_sold
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 -- North America countries
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_revenue_per_track_sold DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: List playlists that contain tracks from at least five different genres.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) >= 5;

question: Count employees who have no manager assigned.
Which tracks appear in the highest number of playlists, and who are the artists of those tracks?
gold_sql:
WITH TrackPlaylistCounts AS (
  SELECT pt.track_id, COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) AS playlist_count
  FROM playlist_track pt
  GROUP BY pt.track_id
), MaxCount AS (
  SELECT MAX(playlist_count) AS max_playlist_count
  FROM TrackPlaylistCounts
)
SELECT t.track_id, t.name AS track_name, a.name AS artist_name, tpc.playlist_count
FROM TrackPlaylistCounts tpc
JOIN MaxCount mc ON tpc.playlist_count = mc.max_playlist_count
JOIN track t ON tpc.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
ORDER BY tpc.playlist_count DESC, t.track_id;

question: What is the total number of tracks per genre and their average price?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks, AVG(t.unit_price) AS average_price
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: For each genre, what is the average track duration and how does it compare to the overall average track length?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreAvg AS (
  SELECT
    g.name AS genre_name,
    AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_duration_seconds
  FROM track t
  JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
  GROUP BY g.name
),
OverallAvg AS (
  SELECT AVG(milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS overall_avg_track_duration_seconds FROM track
)
SELECT
  ga.genre_name,
  ga.avg_track_duration_seconds,
  oa.overall_avg_track_duration_seconds,
  (ga.avg_track_duration_seconds - oa.overall_avg_track_duration_seconds) / oa.overall_avg_track_duration_seconds AS duration_difference_ratio
FROM GenreAvg ga
CROSS JOIN OverallAvg oa
ORDER BY ga.genre_name;

question: How does the average unit price of tracks vary across different media types?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type_name, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY mt.name;

question: List playlists that include tracks purchased by customers from more than three different countries.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) > 3;

question: What is the total revenue per genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) as revenue
            FROM invoice_line il
            JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
            JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
            GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
            ORDER BY revenue DESC

question: What is the longest track duration recorded in milliseconds?
gold_sql: SELECT MAX(milliseconds) AS longest_track_duration_ms FROM track;

question: Which playlists contain the most tracks and what is the average duration of tracks in those playlists?
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistTrackCounts AS (
  SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist p
  JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
  GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
), MaxTrackCount AS (
  SELECT MAX(track_count) AS max_count FROM PlaylistTrackCounts
)
SELECT ptc.playlist_id, ptc.name, ptc.track_count, AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_duration_seconds
FROM PlaylistTrackCounts ptc
JOIN MaxTrackCount mtc ON ptc.track_count = mtc.max_count
JOIN playlist_track pt ON ptc.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY ptc.playlist_id, ptc.name, ptc.track_count;

question: Who are the employees that manage support reps with the lowest average customer invoice totals?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT m.employee_id AS manager_id, m.first_name AS manager_first_name, m.last_name AS manager_last_name
FROM employee e
JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
JOIN customer c ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY m.employee_id, m.first_name, m.last_name
HAVING AVG(i.total) = (
 SELECT MIN(avg_total) FROM (
 SELECT AVG(i2.total) AS avg_total
 FROM employee e2
 JOIN employee m2 ON e2.reports_to = m2.employee_id
 JOIN customer c2 ON c2.support_rep_id = e2.employee_id
 JOIN invoice i2 ON i2.customer_id = c2.customer_id
 GROUP BY m2.employee_id
 ) sub
)
;

question: Show playlists along with the total size in bytes of all their tracks combined.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the average price of all tracks in the store?
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS average_track_price FROM track;

question: Find all playlists whose names start with the letter 'A'.
gold_sql: SELECT playlist_id, name FROM playlist WHERE name LIKE 'A%'

question: Can you show the total number of unique artists featured in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) AS unique_artist_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: How many genres are represented among the tracks?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id) AS genre_count FROM track;

question: What is the maximum track duration in the collection?
gold_sql: SELECT MAX(milliseconds) AS max_track_duration FROM track;

question: How many distinct genres are listed in the genre table?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id) AS distinct_genre_count FROM genre;

question: What is the average unit price of tracks?
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(unit_price) AS average_unit_price FROM track;

question: How many employees work at the company?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee

question: How many invoices were generated by customers in each postal code last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_postal_code, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= date_trunc('year', current_date) - interval '1 year'
  AND invoice_date < date_trunc('year', current_date)
GROUP BY billing_postal_code
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: What is the total revenue generated by customers who have purchased from more than three different artists?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerArtistCount AS (
 SELECT
 i.customer_id,
 COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) AS artist_count
 FROM
 invoice i
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
 JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 -- North America region
 GROUP BY
 i.customer_id
 HAVING
 COUNT(DISTINCT ar.artist_id) > 3
)

SELECT
 SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM
 invoice i
 JOIN CustomerArtistCount cac ON i.customer_id = cac.customer_id;

question: List the top 5 playlists with the highest total number of tracks.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_tracks DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How many playlists contain tracks by the artist with the most albums in the database?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistAlbumCounts AS (
  SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
  FROM album
  GROUP BY artist_id
),
MaxAlbumArtist AS (
  SELECT artist_id
  FROM ArtistAlbumCounts
  WHERE album_count = (SELECT MAX(album_count) FROM ArtistAlbumCounts)
),
ArtistTracks AS (
  SELECT track_id
  FROM track
  WHERE album_id IN (
    SELECT album_id
    FROM album
    WHERE artist_id IN (SELECT artist_id FROM MaxAlbumArtist)
  )
),
PlaylistsWithArtistTracks AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT playlist_id
  FROM playlist_track
  WHERE track_id IN (SELECT track_id FROM ArtistTracks)
)
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlist_count
FROM PlaylistsWithArtistTracks;

question: What is the average invoice total per customer grouped by their support representative?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS support_rep_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total_per_customer
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, support_rep_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which employees have a manager that reports to another manager, forming a multi-level reporting structure?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.title, e.reports_to AS manager_id, m.reports_to AS managers_manager_id
FROM employee e
JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
WHERE m.reports_to IS NOT NULL;

question: Identify support representatives who have customers located in multiple countries and the total revenue generated from these customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) AS country_count, SUM(i.total) AS total_revenue
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT c.country) > 1;

question: Find employees who support customers with invoices containing tracks from more than one genre.
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, i.invoice_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.genre_id) > 1;

question: Which artists have released albums that are included in more than five different playlists?
gold_sql:
SELECT ar.name AS artist_name
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT pt.playlist_id) > 5;

question: Can you identify the top 10 genres by total sales revenue and their average track unit price?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales_revenue,
 AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_unit_price
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY total_sales_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Can you identify customers who purchased tracks from multiple media types and the total tracks bought?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) AS distinct_media_types,
       COUNT(il.invoice_line_id) AS total_tracks_bought
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) > 1;

question: What is the average invoice amount for customers grouped by their assigned support representatives?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_amount
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Which employees support customers with the highest proportion of invoices above the overall average invoice total?
gold_sql:
WITH avg_invoice AS (
 SELECT AVG(total) AS avg_total
 FROM invoice
),
customer_invoice_counts AS (
 SELECT c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id,
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS total_invoices,
 SUM(CASE WHEN i.total > (SELECT avg_total FROM avg_invoice) THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS invoices_above_avg
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, c.customer_id
),
support_rep_proportions AS (
 SELECT support_rep_id,
 SUM(invoices_above_avg) * 1.0 / SUM(total_invoices) AS proportion_above_avg
 FROM customer_invoice_counts
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
),
avg_proportion AS (
 SELECT AVG(proportion_above_avg) AS avg_proportion
 FROM support_rep_proportions
)

SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, srp.proportion_above_avg
FROM employee e
JOIN support_rep_proportions srp ON e.employee_id = srp.support_rep_id
JOIN avg_proportion ap ON srp.proportion_above_avg > ap.avg_proportion
 ;

question: What is the average invoice amount for customers in the United States?
Find the average number of customers supported by employees grouped by their job title.
gold_sql:
SELECT emp_cust.title, AVG(cust_count) AS avg_customers_supported
FROM (
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.title, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS cust_count
 FROM employee e
 LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.title
) AS emp_cust
GROUP BY emp_cust.title;

question: Which media type is the most common among tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM track) AS percentage
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which employees have the highest number of direct reports and how many do they have?
gold_sql:
WITH DirectReportsCount AS (
    SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
    FROM employee e
    LEFT JOIN employee r ON e.employee_id = r.reports_to
    GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
), MaxCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(direct_reports_count) AS max_reports
    FROM DirectReportsCount
)
SELECT d.employee_id, d.first_name, d.last_name, d.direct_reports_count
FROM DirectReportsCount d
JOIN MaxCount m ON d.direct_reports_count = m.max_reports;

question: Which billing city has the highest number of invoices?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Show the average duration of tracks for each genre in the catalog.
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which employees have been hired most recently but already support at least five customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.hire_date, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS supported_customers
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.hire_date
HAVING COUNT(c.customer_id) >= 5
ORDER BY e.hire_date DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: How many customers have a support representative who manages more than 10 customers, and what is their average spending?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS customer_count,
 AVG(i.total) AS average_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.support_rep_id IN (
 SELECT support_rep_id
 FROM customer
 GROUP BY support_rep_id
 HAVING COUNT(customer_id) > 10
)

question: How many layers of management exist between the highest-level manager and the lowest-level support rep?
gold_sql:
WITH RECURSIVE ManagementHierarchy AS (
  -- Start with the highest-level manager (who reports to no one)
  SELECT employee_id, title, reports_to, 0 AS level
  FROM employee
  WHERE reports_to IS NULL
  
  UNION ALL
  
  -- Recursively find employees reporting to the previous level
  SELECT e.employee_id, e.title, e.reports_to, mh.level + 1
  FROM employee e
  INNER JOIN ManagementHierarchy mh ON e.reports_to = mh.employee_id
)

SELECT MAX(level) AS management_layers
FROM ManagementHierarchy;

question: What are the top 10 albums with the largest total size in bytes of all their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY total_bytes DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: How does the average invoice total for customers vary by billing country, and which countries have the highest averages?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: List employees along with the number of unique cities their assigned customers are located in.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.city) AS unique_customer_cities
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which employees have the highest number of direct reports in the company?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(r.employee_id) AS direct_reports_count
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN employee r ON e.employee_id = r.reports_to
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY direct_reports_count DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: List the employees and the number of distinct genres their supported customers have purchased tracks from.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id,
 e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS employee_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS distinct_genre_count
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY distinct_genre_count DESC;

question: Which media types are used in tracks belonging to the Rock genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT mt.name AS media_type_name
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
WHERE g.name = 'Rock';

question: Which playlists contain more than 50 tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(pt.track_id) > 50;

question: Which tracks have generated the highest total sales revenue across all invoices?
gold_sql:
SELECT t.track_id, t.name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales_revenue
FROM track t
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY t.track_id, t.name
ORDER BY total_sales_revenue DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Which albums contain the highest total number of tracks, and what is the total duration of these albums?
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumTrackCounts AS (
    SELECT album_id, COUNT(track_id) AS total_tracks, SUM(milliseconds) AS total_duration
    FROM track
    GROUP BY album_id
), MaxTrackCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(total_tracks) AS max_tracks
    FROM AlbumTrackCounts
)
SELECT a.title, atc.total_tracks, atc.total_duration
FROM AlbumTrackCounts atc
JOIN MaxTrackCount mtc ON atc.total_tracks = mtc.max_tracks
JOIN album a ON atc.album_id = a.album_id;

question: List the top 5 artists with the highest total number of tracks across all their albums.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_tracks DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: What is the average number of tracks per album for each artist?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name, AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_album
FROM (
    SELECT album_id, artist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM album
    JOIN track USING (album_id)
    GROUP BY album_id, artist_id
) a
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY ar.name;

question: How much revenue was generated from different media types?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS revenue
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY revenue DESC;

question: List employees along with the total count of customers assigned to them and how many of those customers have invoices over $1000.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name,
 COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS total_customers,
 COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN i.total > 1000 THEN c.customer_id END) AS customers_with_invoices_over_1000
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Which playlists include tracks from both pop and jazz genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name IN ('Pop', 'Jazz')
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT g.name) = 2;

question: Identify playlists where the average track duration is longer than the average across all tracks.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING AVG(t.milliseconds) > (SELECT AVG(milliseconds) FROM track);

question: Which employees have customers generating the highest average invoice total per customer?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total_per_customer
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total_per_customer DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the monthly sales trend for the last six months by genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 g.name AS genre,
 TO_CHAR(i.invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS monthly_sales
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
