JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
 GROUP BY g.name, year_month
ORDER BY year_month, g.name;

question: Which billing states show the greatest increase in total sales compared to last quarter?
gold_sql:
WITH sales_by_state_and_quarter AS (
 SELECT
 billing_state,
 DATE_TRUNC('quarter', invoice_date) AS quarter,
 SUM(total) AS total_sales
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_state, quarter
),

sales_with_lag AS (
 SELECT
 billing_state,
 quarter,
 total_sales,
 LAG(total_sales) OVER (PARTITION BY billing_state ORDER BY quarter) AS previous_quarter_sales
 FROM sales_by_state_and_quarter
)

SELECT
 billing_state,
 total_sales - previous_quarter_sales AS sales_increase,
 CASE WHEN previous_quarter_sales = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (total_sales - previous_quarter_sales) / previous_quarter_sales::DECIMAL END AS sales_increase_percentage
FROM sales_with_lag
WHERE previous_quarter_sales IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY sales_increase DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the average number of tracks purchased per invoice, broken down by billing country?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  i.billing_country,
  AVG(il.quantity) AS avg_tracks_purchased_per_invoice
FROM
  invoice_line il
JOIN
  invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
WHERE
  i.billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY
  i.billing_country
ORDER BY
  i.billing_country;

question: Calculate the average number of tracks per playlist and identify playlists that have significantly more tracks than this average.
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistTrackCounts AS (
  SELECT playlist_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist_track
  GROUP BY playlist_id
),
AverageTrackCount AS (
  SELECT AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks
  FROM PlaylistTrackCounts
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, ptc.track_count, atc.avg_tracks
FROM playlist p
JOIN PlaylistTrackCounts ptc ON p.playlist_id = ptc.playlist_id
JOIN AverageTrackCount atc ON 1=1
WHERE ptc.track_count > atc.avg_tracks;

question: Determine the top 10 customers by total spending, including their support rep's name.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name AS customer_first_name, c.last_name AS customer_last_name, 
 e.first_name AS support_rep_first_name, e.last_name AS support_rep_last_name, 
 SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_spending DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Which playlists have tracks from albums released by more than two different artists?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) > 2;

question: For each playlist, calculate the total revenue generated by the tracks included (considering sales in invoice lines).
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: Find playlists where the total duration of tracks exceeds the total duration of tracks in any album.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING SUM(t.milliseconds) > (
    SELECT MAX(album_duration) FROM (
        SELECT album_id, SUM(milliseconds) AS album_duration
        FROM track
        GROUP BY album_id
    ) AS album_durations
)

question: Can you provide the total sales and number of invoices for customers in each state?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.state AS customer_state,
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS number_of_invoices,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_sales
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.state
ORDER BY c.state;

question: Which customer groups by city have the highest total purchase amounts?
gold_sql:
WITH city_customer_totals AS (
  SELECT c.city, c.customer_id, SUM(i.total) AS total_purchase_amount
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  GROUP BY c.city, c.customer_id
), city_max_totals AS (
  SELECT city, MAX(total_purchase_amount) AS max_purchase_amount
  FROM city_customer_totals
  GROUP BY city
)
SELECT cct.city, cct.customer_id, cct.total_purchase_amount
FROM city_customer_totals cct
JOIN city_max_totals cmt ON cct.city = cmt.city AND cct.total_purchase_amount = cmt.max_purchase_amount
ORDER BY cct.city;

question: Identify the top 3 genres by total sales revenue in each country.
gold_sql:
WITH GenreSales AS (
  SELECT
    c.country,
    g.name AS genre,
    SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales_revenue
  FROM
    invoice_line il
  JOIN
    invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
  JOIN
    customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
  JOIN
    track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
  JOIN
    genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
  GROUP BY
    c.country, g.name
), RankedGenreSales AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    genre,
    total_sales_revenue,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY total_sales_revenue DESC) AS rank
  FROM
    GenreSales
)
SELECT
  country,
  genre,
  total_sales_revenue
FROM
  RankedGenreSales
WHERE
  rank <= 3
  AND country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
ORDER BY
  country, total_sales_revenue DESC;

question: How many playlists have tracks from more than three different genres?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_more_than_three_genres
FROM (
    SELECT playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    GROUP BY playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 3
) sub;

question: Among employees, who has the highest average invoice total among their customers, excluding those customer invoices under $10?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE i.total >= 10
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the total number of invoices billed from each city and the average invoice total in those cities?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, COUNT(invoice_id) AS total_invoices, AVG(total) AS average_invoice_total
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY billing_city;

question: Which albums have the highest total sales based on invoice lines?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY total_sales DESC;

question: List the top 3 cities by total sales revenue, including a breakdown by genre for those cities.
gold_sql:
WITH CitySales AS (
 SELECT
 c.city,
 SUM(i.total) AS total_sales
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.city
),
TopCities AS (
 SELECT city
 FROM CitySales
 ORDER BY total_sales DESC
 LIMIT 3
),
CityGenreSales AS (
 SELECT
 c.city,
 g.name AS genre,
 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS genre_sales
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
 WHERE c.city IN (SELECT city FROM TopCities)
 GROUP BY c.city, g.name
)
SELECT
 cs.city,
 cs.total_sales,
 cgs.genre,
 cgs.genre_sales,
 cgs.genre_sales / cs.total_sales AS genre_sales_percentage
FROM CitySales cs
JOIN CityGenreSales cgs ON cs.city = cgs.city
WHERE cs.city IN (SELECT city FROM TopCities)
ORDER BY cs.total_sales DESC, cgs.genre_sales DESC;

question: Identify playlists that contain tracks from more than two different albums.
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) > 2;

question: How many invoices have a total greater than $50?
What are the total invoice amounts grouped by month and country for the previous year?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  billing_country,
  SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY year_month, billing_country
ORDER BY year_month, billing_country;

question: Who are the employees that do not support any customers currently?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id

question: List all albums with their artist names

            FROM album
            JOIN artist ON album.artist_id = artist.artist_id

question: Which playlists have the most diverse set of media types among their tracks?

FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, p.name

question: How many customers are assigned to each support representative and what is the total invoice amount generated by those customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS num_customers, COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY num_customers DESC;

question: Identify the top 5 customers by number of purchases in the last six months.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS purchase_count
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY purchase_count DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How does the monthly revenue trend compare between customers from the United States and Canada over the past year?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  to_char(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  billing_country,
  SUM(total) AS monthly_revenue
FROM invoice
WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada')
  AND invoice_date >= current_date - interval '1 year'
GROUP BY year_month, billing_country
ORDER BY year_month, billing_country;

question: Which billing country has the highest average invoice total?

GROUP BY billing_country

question: List the tracks that appear in multiple playlists and their respective playlist names.
gold_sql:
SELECT t.track_id, t.name AS track_name, p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name
FROM track t
JOIN playlist_track pt ON t.track_id = pt.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
WHERE t.track_id IN (
    SELECT track_id
    FROM playlist_track
    GROUP BY track_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT playlist_id) > 1
)
ORDER BY t.track_id, p.playlist_id;

question: Which playlists include tracks from artists with albums in multiple genres, and what is their total track count?
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistMultiGenre AS (
    SELECT a.artist_id
    FROM artist a
    JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
    JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY a.artist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
),
ArtistTracks AS (
    SELECT t.track_id
    FROM track t
    JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
    WHERE al.artist_id IN (SELECT artist_id FROM ArtistMultiGenre)
),
PlaylistArtistTracks AS (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id, pt.track_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    WHERE pt.track_id IN (SELECT track_id FROM ArtistTracks)
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(pat.track_id) AS total_track_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN PlaylistArtistTracks pat ON p.playlist_id = pat.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_track_count DESC;

question: How many playlists include tracks composed by more than five different composers?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS playlists_with_more_than_five_composers
FROM (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    WHERE t.composer IS NOT NULL AND t.composer <> ''
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) > 5
) sub;

question: List playlists that include tracks from genres that have fewer than five tracks in total.
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.genre_id IN (
    SELECT genre_id
    FROM track
    GROUP BY genre_id
    HAVING COUNT(track_id) < 5
)

question: Which albums have generated the highest total revenue in each country?
gold_sql:
WITH RankedAlbums AS (
  SELECT
    c.country,
    a.album_id,
    a.title,
    SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_revenue,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY c.country ORDER BY SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) DESC) AS revenue_rank
  FROM invoice_line il
  JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
  JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
  JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
  JOIN album a ON t.album_id = a.album_id
  GROUP BY c.country, a.album_id, a.title
)
SELECT country, album_id, title, total_revenue
FROM RankedAlbums
WHERE revenue_rank = 1
ORDER BY country;

question: Identify playlists where the sum of unit prices of tracks exceeds $100.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, SUM(t.unit_price) AS total_unit_price
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING SUM(t.unit_price) > 100;

question: Who are the employees that do not report to any manager?
What is the total size in bytes of all tracks in the database?
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(bytes) AS total_bytes FROM track;

question: How many customers are from the USA?
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM customer WHERE country = 'USA'

question: Which customers have invoices with total amounts that increased consecutively for at least three months?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlyTotals AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', invoice_date) AS month,
        SUM(total) AS total_amount
    FROM invoice
    GROUP BY customer_id, month
),
ConsecutiveIncreases AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        month,
        total_amount,
        LAG(total_amount, 1) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_1,
        LAG(total_amount, 2) OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_2
    FROM MonthlyTotals
),
IncreasingStreaks AS (
    SELECT
        customer_id,
        month,
        total_amount,
        CASE
            WHEN total_amount > prev_month_1 AND prev_month_1 > prev_month_2 THEN 1
            ELSE 0
        END AS three_month_increasing
    FROM ConsecutiveIncreases
)
SELECT DISTINCT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
FROM IncreasingStreaks i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE i.three_month_increasing = 1
ORDER BY c.customer_id;

question: What is the total amount spent by each customer?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_amount_spent
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_amount_spent DESC;

question: What is the average invoice amount for customers per country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, AVG(i.total) AS average_invoice_amount
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY c.country
ORDER BY c.country;

question: How many employees have no manager assigned to them?
Which playlists have tracks that belong exclusively to a single media type?

FROM playlist p

JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) = 1;

question: Identify the top 5 countries by total spending on invoices.

question: Show the list of customers with their assigned support rep and their total spending on invoices.
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name || ' ' || c.last_name AS customer_name, 
 e.first_name || ' ' || e.last_name AS support_rep_name, 
 COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS total_spending
FROM customer c
LEFT JOIN employee e ON c.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: Show playlists ranked by the total size in bytes of all included tracks, from highest to lowest.
gold_sql:

FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id

question: Which artists have albums containing tracks priced above $1.50?
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT ar.name AS artist_name
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track tr ON al.album_id = tr.album_id
WHERE tr.unit_price > 1.50;

question: Which billing postal codes have shown the largest growth in total invoice amounts compared to the previous year?
gold_sql:
WITH yearly_totals AS (
 SELECT
 billing_postal_code,
 TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY') AS year,
 SUM(total) AS total_amount
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_postal_code, year
),

growth AS (
 SELECT
 current.billing_postal_code,
 current.year,
 current.total_amount AS current_year_amount,
 previous.total_amount AS previous_year_amount,
 CASE 
 WHEN previous.total_amount IS NULL THEN NULL
 ELSE (current.total_amount - previous.total_amount) / previous.total_amount::numeric
 END AS growth_rate
 FROM yearly_totals current
 LEFT JOIN yearly_totals previous
 ON current.billing_postal_code = previous.billing_postal_code
WHERE CAST(current.year AS INTEGER) = CAST(previous.year AS INTEGER) + 1
)

SELECT billing_postal_code, year, current_year_amount, previous_year_amount, growth_rate
FROM growth
WHERE growth_rate IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY growth_rate DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Identify the top 3 genres by average track length.
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_length_seconds
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY avg_track_length_seconds DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the distribution of albums per artist and how many tracks each album contains on average?
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumTrackCounts AS (
    SELECT album_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
    FROM track
    GROUP BY album_id
),
ArtistAlbumCounts AS (
    SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
    FROM album
    GROUP BY artist_id
),
AlbumTrackAvg AS (
    SELECT artist_id, AVG(track_count) AS avg_tracks_per_album
    FROM album a
    JOIN AlbumTrackCounts atc ON a.album_id = atc.album_id
    GROUP BY artist_id
)
SELECT 
    a.artist_id,
    ar.name AS artist_name,
    a.album_count,
    at.avg_tracks_per_album
FROM ArtistAlbumCounts a
JOIN AlbumTrackAvg at ON a.artist_id = at.artist_id
JOIN artist ar ON a.artist_id = ar.artist_id
ORDER BY a.album_count DESC;

question: Which countries have more than 5 customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT country, COUNT(*) as customer_count
            FROM customer
            GROUP BY country
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
            ORDER BY customer_count DESC

question: How many tracks does each playlist contain, and which playlists have more than 50 tracks?
gold_sql:

FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(pt.track_id) > 50;

question: What is the total amount spent by customers who purchased tracks from more than three different genres?
gold_sql:

FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 3;

question: Which media type features the longest average track duration?

FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY avg_duration_milliseconds DESC

question: Which employee has the highest total invoice value from their assigned customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_value
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_value DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: What is the distribution of genres within each playlist, and which genre is most common per playlist?
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistGenreCounts AS (
  SELECT
    pt.playlist_id,
    t.genre_id,
    COUNT(*) AS genre_track_count
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, t.genre_id
),
PlaylistTotalCounts AS (
  SELECT
    playlist_id,
    SUM(genre_track_count) AS total_tracks
  FROM PlaylistGenreCounts
  GROUP BY playlist_id
),
PlaylistGenreDistribution AS (
  SELECT
    pgc.playlist_id,
    pgc.genre_id,
    pgc.genre_track_count,
    pgc.genre_track_count * 1.0 / ptc.total_tracks AS genre_percentage
  FROM PlaylistGenreCounts pgc
  JOIN PlaylistTotalCounts ptc ON pgc.playlist_id = ptc.playlist_id
),
MostCommonGenrePerPlaylist AS (
  SELECT
    playlist_id,
    genre_id,
    genre_track_count,
    genre_percentage,
    RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY playlist_id ORDER BY genre_track_count DESC) AS rank
  FROM PlaylistGenreDistribution
)
SELECT
  p.playlist_id,
  p.name AS playlist_name,
  g.genre_id,
  g.name AS genre_name,
  mc.genre_track_count,
  mc.genre_percentage
FROM MostCommonGenrePerPlaylist mc
JOIN playlist p ON mc.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
JOIN genre g ON mc.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE mc.rank = 1
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the total invoice amount billed to customers in each city?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city AS city, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: How many tracks are composed by each composer and what is the average unit price for their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT composer, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count, AVG(unit_price) AS avg_unit_price
FROM track
GROUP BY composer

question: Which playlists include tracks composed by the largest number of different composers?
gold_sql:
WITH ComposerCountByPlaylist AS (
    SELECT
        pt.playlist_id,
        COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) AS distinct_composer_count
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    WHERE t.composer IS NOT NULL AND t.composer <> ''
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
), MaxComposerCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(distinct_composer_count) AS max_composer_count
    FROM ComposerCountByPlaylist
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, c.distinct_composer_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN ComposerCountByPlaylist c ON p.playlist_id = c.playlist_id
JOIN MaxComposerCount m ON c.distinct_composer_count = m.max_composer_count;

question: Find the top 3 best-selling tracks in each genre
gold_sql:
SELECT genre_name, track_name, tracks_sold FROM (
                SELECT
                    g.name as genre_name,
                    t.name as track_name,
                    COUNT(il.invoice_line_id) as tracks_sold,
                    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                        PARTITION BY g.genre_id
                        ORDER BY COUNT(il.invoice_line_id) DESC
                    ) as rn
                FROM genre g
                JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
                JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
                GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name, t.track_id, t.name
            ) ranked
            WHERE rn <= 3
            ORDER BY genre_name, tracks_sold DESC

question: In which media type do tracks have the greatest variance in unit price, and what is the average price in that media type?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type_name, VAR_SAMP(t.unit_price) AS price_variance, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_price
FROM track t
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY price_variance DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which playlists contain more than 20 tracks and what is the average track duration in those playlists?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(pt.track_id) > 20;

question: Identify employees who do not manage any other employees.
gold_sql:
SELECT employee_id, first_name, last_name
FROM employee
WHERE employee_id NOT IN (
    SELECT reports_to 
    FROM employee 
    WHERE reports_to IS NOT NULL
);

question: What is the distinct count of composers represented in each playlist and which playlist has the highest count?
gold_sql:
WITH ComposerCounts AS (
    SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) AS distinct_composer_count
    FROM playlist p
    JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    WHERE t.composer IS NOT NULL AND t.composer <> ''
    GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
)
SELECT playlist_id, playlist_name, distinct_composer_count
FROM ComposerCounts
WHERE distinct_composer_count = (SELECT MAX(distinct_composer_count) FROM ComposerCounts);

question: List the playlists along with the total size in bytes of all the tracks they include.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.name AS playlist_name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_size_bytes
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.name
ORDER BY p.name;

question: What is the average invoice total for each country?
What is the average total amount spent per invoice?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(total) AS average_total_amount_spent_per_invoice
FROM invoice

question: For each playlist, what is the total size in bytes and the number of unique genres represented?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name,
       SUM(t.bytes) AS total_size_bytes,
       COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS unique_genre_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: Find the playlists with the smallest and largest average track duration and provide their names and average durations.
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
),
MinMaxAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT
        MIN(avg_duration) AS min_avg_duration,
        MAX(avg_duration) AS max_avg_duration
    FROM PlaylistAvgDuration
)
SELECT p.name, pad.avg_duration
FROM PlaylistAvgDuration pad
JOIN playlist p ON pad.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
JOIN MinMaxAvgDuration mm ON pad.avg_duration = mm.min_avg_duration OR pad.avg_duration = mm.max_avg_duration;

question: List the artists whose total number of tracks exceeds the average number of tracks per artist.
gold_sql:
WITH ArtistTrackCounts AS (
  SELECT ar.artist_id, ar.name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
  FROM artist ar
  JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
),
AverageTracks AS (
  SELECT AVG(total_tracks) AS avg_tracks
  FROM ArtistTrackCounts
)
SELECT atc.name, atc.total_tracks
FROM ArtistTrackCounts atc, AverageTracks at
WHERE atc.total_tracks > at.avg_tracks
ORDER BY atc.total_tracks DESC;

question: List the albums where all tracks belong to the same genre and have a duration above the average duration for that genre.
gold_sql:
WITH GenreAvgDuration AS (
  SELECT genre_id, AVG(milliseconds) AS avg_duration
  FROM track
  GROUP BY genre_id
),
AlbumTrackInfo AS (
  SELECT album_id, genre_id, milliseconds
  FROM track
),
AlbumGenreCheck AS (
  SELECT album_id,
         COUNT(DISTINCT genre_id) AS distinct_genres,
         COUNT(*) AS total_tracks
  FROM AlbumTrackInfo
  GROUP BY album_id
),
AlbumTracksAboveAvg AS (
  SELECT a.album_id
  FROM AlbumTrackInfo a
  JOIN GenreAvgDuration g ON a.genre_id = g.genre_id
  WHERE a.milliseconds > g.avg_duration
  GROUP BY a.album_id
  HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT total_tracks FROM AlbumGenreCheck WHERE album_id = a.album_id)
)
SELECT al.album_id, al.title
FROM album al
JOIN AlbumGenreCheck agc ON al.album_id = agc.album_id
JOIN AlbumTracksAboveAvg ata ON al.album_id = ata.album_id
WHERE agc.distinct_genres = 1;

question: Find the number of invoices and total revenue for each billing postal code.
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_postal_code, COUNT(invoice_id) AS number_of_invoices, SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_postal_code
ORDER BY billing_postal_code;

question: What are the names of playlists that include tracks priced above the average unit price?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.unit_price > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track);

question: What is the total revenue generated from customers in each billing state?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY total_revenue DESC;

question: How many playlists exist with the name containing 'Rock'?
Identify the customers whose average invoice total exceeds their support representative's average supported customer invoice total.
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerAvgInvoice AS (
 SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.support_rep_id,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.support_rep_id
),
SupportRepAvgInvoice AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 AVG(i.total) AS avg_supported_customer_invoice_total
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id
)
SELECT
 c.customer_id,
 c.first_name,
 c.last_name,
 ca.avg_invoice_total,
 sra.avg_supported_customer_invoice_total
FROM customer c
JOIN CustomerAvgInvoice ca ON c.customer_id = ca.customer_id
JOIN SupportRepAvgInvoice sra ON ca.support_rep_id = sra.support_rep_id
WHERE ca.avg_invoice_total > sra.avg_supported_customer_invoice_total
 -- Filtering for North America region (USA as example)
ORDER BY ca.avg_invoice_total DESC;

question: Find customers who have never made a purchase
gold_sql:
SELECT first_name, last_name
            FROM customer
            WHERE customer_id NOT IN (
                SELECT DISTINCT customer_id FROM invoice
            )

question: What is the average invoice amount by billing city?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, AVG(total) AS average_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY billing_city;

question: Which countries have the highest average invoice amount over the past six months?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, AVG(total) AS avg_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY avg_invoice_amount DESC;

question: What is the distribution of employees by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS employee_count, COUNT(*) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM employee) AS employee_percentage
FROM employee
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY employee_count DESC;

question: Which customers have spent the most money on invoices grouped by country?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.country, c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_spent
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY c.country, c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY c.country, total_spent DESC;

question: Can you list employees by the number of customers they support and the cumulative invoice total of those customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(DISTINCT c.customer_id) AS number_of_customers, COALESCE(SUM(i.total), 0) AS cumulative_invoice_total
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
LEFT JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY number_of_customers DESC, cumulative_invoice_total DESC;

question: How many invoices were issued each month over the last year, and what was the total revenue per month?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count,
  SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY year_month
ORDER BY year_month;

question: List the top five employees based on the total invoice amounts of their assigned customers.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: List employees who do not have any customers assigned to them and their respective titles.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, e.title
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
WHERE c.customer_id IS NULL;

question: What is the distribution of track durations by genre for the classical genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  t.genre_id,
  g.name AS genre_name,
  CASE
    WHEN t.milliseconds < 180000 THEN 'Short (<3 min)'
    WHEN t.milliseconds BETWEEN 180000 AND 300000 THEN 'Medium (3-5 min)'
    ELSE 'Long (>5 min)'
  END AS duration_category,
  COUNT(*) AS track_count,
  COUNT(*) * 1.0 / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER () AS track_percentage
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE g.name = 'Classical'
GROUP BY t.genre_id, g.name, duration_category
ORDER BY duration_category;

question: What is the total sales amount for each billing country in the last year?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_country, SUM(total) AS total_sales_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '1 year'
  AND invoice_date < DATE_TRUNC('year', CURRENT_DATE)
GROUP BY billing_country
ORDER BY total_sales_amount DESC;

question: Identify playlists that include tracks from more than three different albums.
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN playlist p ON pt.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.album_id) > 3;

question: Which employees are responsible for customers with the highest average invoice totals?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: For each artist, what are the minimum, maximum, and average track durations?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, a.name AS artist_name,
       MIN(t.milliseconds) AS min_duration,
       MAX(t.milliseconds) AS max_duration,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY a.name;

question: Find the average length of tracks and total sales generated for each album.
gold_sql:
SELECT
  a.album_id,
  a.title AS album_title,
  AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_length_seconds,
  SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY a.album_id;

question: For each playlist, show the number of unique artists whose tracks appear on that playlist.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) AS unique_artist_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: For each artist, what is the average duration of their tracks and how does it compare to the overall average track duration?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  ar.artist_id,
  ar.name AS artist_name,
  AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration_artist,
  (SELECT AVG(milliseconds) FROM track) AS avg_track_duration_overall,
  AVG(t.milliseconds) / (SELECT AVG(milliseconds) FROM track) AS duration_ratio
FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY ar.artist_id;

question: Are there any playlists where all tracks belong to the same media type? If so, list those playlists.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) = 1;

question: For each employee, how many customers have purchased tracks from more than two different genres?
gold_sql:
WITH customer_genre_counts AS (
 SELECT c.customer_id, c.support_rep_id AS employee_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count
 FROM customer c
 JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 JOIN invoice_line il ON i.invoice_id = il.invoice_id
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.support_rep_id
 HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 2
)
SELECT employee_id, COUNT(customer_id) AS customers_with_more_than_two_genres
FROM customer_genre_counts
GROUP BY employee_id
ORDER BY customers_with_more_than_two_genres DESC;

question: Identify playlists that include tracks from at least five different genres and report the total track count for those playlists.
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS genre_count, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) >= 5;

question: Identify the top 3 states by total invoice amounts in the past year.
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_state, SUM(total) AS total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
GROUP BY billing_state
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: What is the average total invoice amount grouped by the support rep's manager?
gold_sql:
SELECT mgr.first_name || ' ' || mgr.last_name AS manager_name,
 AVG(inv.total) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM invoice inv
JOIN customer cust ON inv.customer_id = cust.customer_id
JOIN employee rep ON cust.support_rep_id = rep.employee_id
JOIN employee mgr ON rep.reports_to = mgr.employee_id
 GROUP BY mgr.first_name, mgr.last_name
ORDER BY avg_total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: How many albums does each artist have, and what is the average price of tracks in those albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.artist_id, ar.name AS artist_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.album_id) AS album_count, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM artist ar
JOIN album a ON ar.artist_id = a.artist_id
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY album_count DESC;

question: How does the total quantity of tracks sold vary by media type and genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, g.name AS genre, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity_sold
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY mt.name, g.name
ORDER BY mt.name, g.name;

question: Determine which playlists include tracks that were never purchased by any customer.
gold_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
LEFT JOIN invoice_line il ON pt.track_id = il.track_id
WHERE il.track_id IS NULL;

question: For each media type, what is the distribution of track unit prices categorized into low, medium, and high brackets based on quartiles?
gold_sql:
WITH PriceQuartiles AS (
  SELECT
    media_type_id,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS Q1,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.50) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS Q2,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS Q3
  FROM track
  GROUP BY media_type_id
),
PriceDistribution AS (
  SELECT
    t.media_type_id,
    CASE
      WHEN t.unit_price <= pq.Q1 THEN 'Low'
      WHEN t.unit_price <= pq.Q3 THEN 'Medium'
      ELSE 'High'
    END AS price_bracket
  FROM track t
  JOIN PriceQuartiles pq ON t.media_type_id = pq.media_type_id
),
BracketCounts AS (
  SELECT
    media_type_id,
    price_bracket,
    COUNT(*) AS count
  FROM PriceDistribution
  GROUP BY media_type_id, price_bracket
),
TotalCounts AS (
  SELECT
    media_type_id,
    COUNT(*) AS total_count
  FROM track
  GROUP BY media_type_id
)
SELECT
  mt.name AS media_type,
  bc.price_bracket,
  bc.count,
  bc.count::decimal / tc.total_count AS percentage
FROM BracketCounts bc
JOIN TotalCounts tc ON bc.media_type_id = tc.media_type_id
JOIN media_type mt ON bc.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
ORDER BY mt.name, bc.price_bracket;

question: Which genres have the highest total sales revenue from customers located in countries with above-average invoice counts?
gold_sql:
WITH CountryInvoiceCounts AS (
  SELECT billing_country, COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count
  FROM invoice
  GROUP BY billing_country
),
AverageInvoiceCount AS (
  SELECT AVG(invoice_count) AS avg_invoice_count
  FROM CountryInvoiceCounts
),
CountriesAboveAverage AS (
  SELECT billing_country
  FROM CountryInvoiceCounts, AverageInvoiceCount
  WHERE invoice_count > avg_invoice_count
),
CustomerCountries AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT customer_id
  FROM customer
  WHERE country IN (SELECT billing_country FROM CountriesAboveAverage)
),
GenreSales AS (
  SELECT g.name AS genre_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
  FROM invoice_line il
  JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
  JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
  JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
  JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
  WHERE c.country IN (SELECT billing_country FROM CountriesAboveAverage)
  GROUP BY g.name
)
SELECT genre_name, total_sales
FROM GenreSales
ORDER BY total_sales DESC;

question: What is the average total spent by customers who have more than 3 invoices?
gold_sql:
SELECT AVG(customer_total) AS avg_total_spent
FROM (
    SELECT customer_id, SUM(total) AS customer_total
    FROM invoice
    GROUP BY customer_id
    HAVING COUNT(invoice_id) > 3
) AS customer_totals;

question: How many tracks does each playlist contain, and what is the average track duration per playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id,
       COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_track_duration_milliseconds
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
ORDER BY pt.playlist_id;

question: Find the cities with the largest growth in total invoice amounts compared to the previous year.
gold_sql:
WITH yearly_city_revenue AS (
 SELECT
 EXTRACT(YEAR FROM invoice_date) AS year,
 billing_city AS city,
 SUM(total) AS total_revenue
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY year, city
),
revenue_growth AS (
 SELECT
 current.year,
 current.city,
 current.total_revenue AS current_year_revenue,
 previous.total_revenue AS previous_year_revenue,
 CASE
 WHEN previous.total_revenue IS NULL THEN NULL
 ELSE (current.total_revenue - previous.total_revenue) / previous.total_revenue
 END AS revenue_growth_rate
 FROM yearly_city_revenue current
 LEFT JOIN yearly_city_revenue previous
 ON current.city = previous.city AND current.year = previous.year + 1
)
SELECT year, city, current_year_revenue, previous_year_revenue, revenue_growth_rate
FROM revenue_growth
WHERE revenue_growth_rate IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY revenue_growth_rate DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the total number of employees located in the United States?
Among all playlists, which have the highest average track duration and what genres are most represented in those playlists?
gold_sql:
WITH PlaylistAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id,
           AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
),
MaxAvgDuration AS (
    SELECT MAX(avg_duration) AS max_avg_duration
    FROM PlaylistAvgDuration
),
TopPlaylists AS (
    SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, pad.avg_duration
    FROM playlist p
    JOIN PlaylistAvgDuration pad ON p.playlist_id = pad.playlist_id
    JOIN MaxAvgDuration mad ON pad.avg_duration = mad.max_avg_duration
),
PlaylistGenres AS (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id, g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(*) AS genre_count
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, g.genre_id, g.name
),
TopPlaylistGenres AS (
    SELECT pg.playlist_id, pg.genre_id, pg.genre_name, pg.genre_count,
           CAST(pg.genre_count AS FLOAT) / SUM(pg.genre_count) OVER (PARTITION BY pg.playlist_id) AS genre_percentage
    FROM PlaylistGenres pg
    WHERE pg.playlist_id IN (SELECT playlist_id FROM TopPlaylists)
)
SELECT tp.playlist_id, tp.name AS playlist_name, tp.avg_duration, tpg.genre_name, tpg.genre_count, tpg.genre_percentage
FROM TopPlaylists tp
JOIN TopPlaylistGenres tpg ON tp.playlist_id = tpg.playlist_id
ORDER BY tp.playlist_id, tpg.genre_count DESC;

question: Which albums have an average track price higher than the overall average track price across all albums?
gold_sql:
SELECT album.album_id, album.title, AVG(track.unit_price) AS avg_album_track_price
FROM album album
JOIN track track ON album.album_id = track.album_id
GROUP BY album.album_id, album.title
HAVING AVG(track.unit_price) > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track);

question: Find playlists with the highest total size in bytes of all tracks combined.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, SUM(t.bytes) AS total_bytes
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY total_bytes DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: For each genre, what is the difference between the longest track duration and the shortest track duration?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, 
       MAX(t.milliseconds) - MIN(t.milliseconds) AS duration_difference_milliseconds
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which albums have contributed the most to the overall invoice revenue?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS album_revenue, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) / (SELECT SUM(total) FROM invoice) AS revenue_percentage
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
ORDER BY album_revenue DESC;

question: Identify playlists where all tracks are composed by the same individual or group.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.composer) = 1 AND COUNT(t.track_id) > 0;

question: For each employee, what is the average total invoice amount from the customers they support?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_total_invoice_amount
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY e.employee_id;

question: Show employees who report to Andrew Adams

            JOIN employee m ON e.reports_to = m.employee_id
            WHERE m.first_name = 'Andrew' AND m.last_name = 'Adams'

question: Which support representatives manage customers who generated invoice totals above the average?

FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name;

question: How much revenue has each billing state generated in the past quarter?

FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '3 months'

question: Which customers have the largest difference between their highest and lowest invoice totals?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, (MAX(i.total) - MIN(i.total)) AS difference
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY difference DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: What is the distribution of total spending per customer divided into quartiles within each country?
gold_sql:
WITH CustomerTotalSpending AS (
  SELECT
    c.customer_id,
    c.country,
    SUM(i.total) AS total_spending
  FROM customer c
  JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
  WHERE c.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
  GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.country
),
Quartiles AS (
  SELECT
    customer_id,
    country,
    total_spending,
    NTILE(4) OVER (PARTITION BY country ORDER BY total_spending) AS spending_quartile
  FROM CustomerTotalSpending
),
QuartileDistribution AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    spending_quartile,
    COUNT(*) AS customer_count
  FROM Quartiles
  GROUP BY country, spending_quartile
),
CountryTotals AS (
  SELECT
    country,
    SUM(customer_count) AS total_customers
  FROM QuartileDistribution
  GROUP BY country
)
SELECT
  qd.country,
  qd.spending_quartile,
  qd.customer_count,
  CAST(qd.customer_count AS FLOAT) / ct.total_customers AS percentage_of_customers
FROM QuartileDistribution qd
JOIN CountryTotals ct ON qd.country = ct.country
ORDER BY qd.country, qd.spending_quartile;

question: Find genres with the highest total number of tracks and their average duration in milliseconds.
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks, AVG(t.milliseconds) AS avg_duration_milliseconds
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
ORDER BY total_tracks DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Find playlists with tracks that belong to more than three different media types and list those media types.
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, mt.media_type_id, mt.name AS media_type_name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
WHERE p.playlist_id IN (
    SELECT pt.playlist_id
    FROM playlist_track pt
    JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
    GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) > 3
)
ORDER BY p.playlist_id, mt.media_type_id;

question: Which genres have the lowest average unit price for tracks but the highest total number of distinct tracks?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreStats AS (
    SELECT g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name,
           AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_unit_price,
           COUNT(DISTINCT t.track_id) AS distinct_track_count
    FROM genre g
    JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
    GROUP BY g.genre_id, g.name
),
RankedGenres AS (
    SELECT *,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY avg_unit_price ASC) AS price_rank,
           RANK() OVER (ORDER BY distinct_track_count DESC) AS track_count_rank
    FROM GenreStats
)
SELECT genre_id, genre_name, avg_unit_price, distinct_track_count
FROM RankedGenres
WHERE price_rank = 1 AND track_count_rank = 1;

question: Can you identify the months where total invoice sales exceeded the monthly average for the entire year?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlySales AS (
 SELECT
 TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
 SUM(total) AS total_sales
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY year_month
),
YearlyAverage AS (
 SELECT AVG(total_sales) AS avg_monthly_sales
 FROM MonthlySales
)
SELECT
 year_month,
 total_sales
FROM MonthlySales, YearlyAverage
WHERE total_sales > avg_monthly_sales
ORDER BY year_month;

question: What is the total invoice amount grouped by billing city and ordered by highest revenue?

question: Find the employees who support customers generating the highest cumulative invoice totals.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS cumulative_invoice_total
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: How does the total spending of customers compare between different regions or states?
gold_sql:
WITH region_spending AS (
 SELECT billing_state AS state, billing_country AS country, SUM(total) AS total_spending
 FROM invoice
 GROUP BY billing_state, billing_country
),
region_totals AS (
 SELECT country, SUM(total_spending) AS country_total
 FROM region_spending
 GROUP BY country
)
SELECT 
 rs.state,
 rs.country,
 rs.total_spending,
 rs.total_spending / rt.country_total AS spending_percentage
FROM region_spending rs
JOIN region_totals rt ON rs.country = rt.country
ORDER BY rs.country, rs.total_spending DESC;

question: Which playlists contain tracks from albums whose total track count exceeds the average album size?
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumTrackCounts AS (
  SELECT album_id, COUNT(track_id) AS track_count
  FROM track
  GROUP BY album_id
),
AverageAlbumSize AS (
  SELECT AVG(track_count) AS avg_track_count
  FROM AlbumTrackCounts
),
AlbumsAboveAverage AS (
  SELECT album_id
  FROM AlbumTrackCounts, AverageAlbumSize
  WHERE track_count > avg_track_count
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.album_id IN (SELECT album_id FROM AlbumsAboveAverage);

question: How many tracks have a duration longer than 5 minutes?
Show the total revenue generated by each artist from track sales.

JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id

question: Which employees have the highest number of invoices associated with their assigned customers?

question: Can you find support representatives whose customers have increasing monthly invoice totals over the past year?
gold_sql:
WITH MonthlyTotals AS (
 SELECT
 c.support_rep_id,
 i.customer_id,
 DATE_TRUNC('month', i.invoice_date) AS month,
 SUM(i.total) AS monthly_total
 FROM invoice i
 JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
 GROUP BY c.support_rep_id, i.customer_id, DATE_TRUNC('month', i.invoice_date)
),
RankedTotals AS (
 SELECT
 support_rep_id,
 customer_id,
 month,
 monthly_total,
 ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY month) AS rn
 FROM MonthlyTotals
),
IncreasingTrend AS (
 SELECT
 rt1.support_rep_id,
 rt1.customer_id
 FROM RankedTotals rt1
 JOIN RankedTotals rt2 ON rt1.customer_id = rt2.customer_id AND rt1.rn = rt2.rn - 1
 WHERE rt2.monthly_total > rt1.monthly_total
 GROUP BY rt1.support_rep_id, rt1.customer_id
 HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT MAX(rn) - 1 FROM RankedTotals WHERE customer_id = rt1.customer_id)
)
SELECT DISTINCT
 e.employee_id AS support_rep_id,
 e.first_name,
 e.last_name
FROM IncreasingTrend it
JOIN employee e ON it.support_rep_id = e.employee_id
 ORDER BY e.last_name, e.first_name;

question: For each playlist, what is the total combined size in bytes of all its tracks?

question: Identify the top 5 support representatives by the total sales revenue of their customers in the past six months.

 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name

question: For each support representative, what is the average total invoice amount of their assigned customers?

question: Which employees have the title 'IT Manager'?
Which genres generate the highest average revenue per track sold?

 SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) / SUM(il.quantity) AS avg_revenue_per_track_sold
FROM genre g
JOIN track t ON g.genre_id = t.genre_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id

 GROUP BY g.name

question: What is the distribution of support representatives by country and the average number of customers they manage?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  e.country AS support_rep_country,
  COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS num_support_reps,
  COUNT(c.customer_id) * 1.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS avg_customers_per_rep,
  COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT employee_id) FROM employee WHERE title LIKE '%Support%' AND country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')) AS pct_support_reps
FROM employee e
LEFT JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
WHERE e.title LIKE '%Support%' AND e.country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY e.country
ORDER BY e.country;

question: What is the total size in bytes of tracks grouped by genre, and which genres have the largest average track size?

question: Identify the top 5 customers by total invoice amount over the last year.

FROM invoice i

question: Which artist has the highest average track duration across all their albums?

FROM artist ar
JOIN album al ON ar.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY ar.artist_id, ar.name
ORDER BY avg_track_duration DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which genres have the highest median track price, and how do they compare to the overall median track price?
gold_sql:
WITH GenreMedianPrice AS (
  SELECT
    genre_id,
    PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS median_price
  FROM track
  GROUP BY genre_id
),
OverallMedianPrice AS (
  SELECT PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY unit_price) AS overall_median_price
  FROM track
)
SELECT
  g.name AS genre_name,
  gmp.median_price,
  omp.overall_median_price,
  (gmp.median_price - omp.overall_median_price) / omp.overall_median_price AS median_price_difference_ratio
FROM GenreMedianPrice gmp
JOIN genre g ON gmp.genre_id = g.genre_id
CROSS JOIN OverallMedianPrice omp
ORDER BY gmp.median_price DESC;

question: What is the total sales revenue generated by each customer?

FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY total_sales_revenue DESC;

question: What is the average unit price of the tracks in each playlist and which playlist has the highest average?

JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name

question: How many invoices were generated each month in the previous year?

question: What is the total quantity of tracks purchased per genre by customers in the United States?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name, SUM(il.quantity) AS total_quantity
FROM invoice_line il
JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
WHERE c.country = 'USA'
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY total_quantity DESC;

question: What is the most popular artist in each country by sales?
gold_sql:
SELECT country, artist_name, total_sales FROM (
                SELECT
                    c.country,
                    ar.name as artist_name,
                    SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) as total_sales,
                    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
                        PARTITION BY c.country
                        ORDER BY SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) DESC
                    ) as rn
                FROM invoice_line il
                JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
                JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
                JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
                JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
                JOIN artist ar ON al.artist_id = ar.artist_id
                GROUP BY c.country, ar.artist_id, ar.name
            ) ranked
            WHERE rn = 1
            ORDER BY country

question: Which playlists include tracks from artists who have released albums in more than one genre?

WITH ArtistGenres AS (
    SELECT 
        a.artist_id,

    FROM artist a
    JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
    JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
    GROUP BY a.artist_id
    HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) > 1
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN ArtistGenres ag ON al.artist_id = ag.artist_id
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

 GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Identify the albums whose average track price is higher than the overall average track price in the database.
gold_sql:
SELECT a.album_id, a.title, AVG(t.unit_price) AS avg_track_price
FROM album a
JOIN track t ON a.album_id = t.album_id
GROUP BY a.album_id, a.title
HAVING AVG(t.unit_price) > (SELECT AVG(unit_price) FROM track);

question: What is the total amount spent by customers in each billing city?
gold_sql:
SELECT billing_city, SUM(total) AS total_amount_spent
FROM invoice
GROUP BY billing_city
ORDER BY total_amount_spent DESC;

question: What are the top three playlists with the greatest number of unique genres represented by their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name, COUNT(DISTINCT t.genre_id) AS unique_genre_count
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
GROUP BY p.playlist_id, p.name
ORDER BY unique_genre_count DESC
LIMIT 3;

question: Can you provide the count of unique artists represented in each playlist?
gold_sql:
SELECT pt.playlist_id, COUNT(DISTINCT a.artist_id) AS unique_artist_count
FROM playlist_track pt
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
JOIN album al ON t.album_id = al.album_id
JOIN artist a ON al.artist_id = a.artist_id
GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
ORDER BY pt.playlist_id;

question: How does the total number of tracks differ between albums released by artists with more than five albums and those with fewer?
gold_sql:
WITH artist_album_counts AS (
  SELECT artist_id, COUNT(album_id) AS album_count
  FROM album
  GROUP BY artist_id
),
artist_track_counts AS (
  SELECT a.artist_id, a.album_count, COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
  FROM artist_album_counts a
  JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
  JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
  GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.album_count
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN album_count > 5 THEN 'More than 5 albums' ELSE '5 or fewer albums' END AS album_group,
  SUM(total_tracks) AS total_tracks
FROM artist_track_counts
GROUP BY album_group;

question: What is the sum of total invoice amounts and count of invoices by customer city in descending order of revenue?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.city AS customer_city, 
 SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_amount, 
 COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
 GROUP BY c.city
ORDER BY total_invoice_amount DESC;

question: Can you show the top five customers with the highest average invoice total in the last six months?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, AVG(i.total) AS avg_invoice_total
FROM invoice i
JOIN customer c ON i.customer_id = c.customer_id
WHERE i.invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '6 months'
GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name
ORDER BY avg_invoice_total DESC
LIMIT 5;

question: How many employees have a fax number listed?
gold_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS employees_with_fax
FROM employee
WHERE fax IS NOT NULL AND fax <> ''

question: What is the distribution of tracks per genre within each playlist for the top 10 playlists by track count?
gold_sql:
WITH TopPlaylists AS (
  SELECT pt.playlist_id, COUNT(pt.track_id) AS track_count
  FROM playlist_track pt
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id
  ORDER BY track_count DESC
  LIMIT 10
),
PlaylistGenreCounts AS (
  SELECT pt.playlist_id, t.genre_id, COUNT(*) AS genre_track_count
  FROM playlist_track pt
  JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
  WHERE pt.playlist_id IN (SELECT playlist_id FROM TopPlaylists)
  GROUP BY pt.playlist_id, t.genre_id
),
PlaylistTotalTracks AS (
  SELECT playlist_id, SUM(genre_track_count) AS total_tracks
  FROM PlaylistGenreCounts
  GROUP BY playlist_id
)
SELECT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name, g.genre_id, g.name AS genre_name,
       pgc.genre_track_count, 
       CAST(pgc.genre_track_count AS FLOAT) / pt.total_tracks AS genre_track_percentage
FROM PlaylistGenreCounts pgc
JOIN PlaylistTotalTracks pt ON pgc.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN playlist p ON pgc.playlist_id = p.playlist_id
JOIN genre g ON pgc.genre_id = g.genre_id
ORDER BY pgc.playlist_id, genre_track_percentage DESC;

question: Which playlists contain tracks from the most frequently purchased albums?
gold_sql:
WITH AlbumPurchaseCounts AS (
    SELECT t.album_id, COUNT(*) AS purchase_count
    FROM invoice_line il
    JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
    GROUP BY t.album_id
),
MaxPurchaseCount AS (
    SELECT MAX(purchase_count) AS max_count
    FROM AlbumPurchaseCounts
),
MostPurchasedAlbums AS (
    SELECT album_id
    FROM AlbumPurchaseCounts
    WHERE purchase_count = (SELECT max_count FROM MaxPurchaseCount)
)
SELECT DISTINCT p.playlist_id, p.name AS playlist_name
FROM playlist p
JOIN playlist_track pt ON p.playlist_id = pt.playlist_id
JOIN track t ON pt.track_id = t.track_id
WHERE t.album_id IN (SELECT album_id FROM MostPurchasedAlbums)
ORDER BY p.playlist_id;

question: What is the percentage of invoices that include tracks from multiple media types?
gold_sql:
WITH invoice_media_types AS (
 SELECT il.invoice_id, COUNT(DISTINCT t.media_type_id) AS distinct_media_types
 FROM invoice_line il
 JOIN track t ON il.track_id = t.track_id
 JOIN invoice i ON il.invoice_id = i.invoice_id
 GROUP BY il.invoice_id
)
SELECT 
 CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN distinct_media_types > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS FLOAT) / COUNT(*) AS percentage_multi_media_type_invoices
FROM invoice_media_types;

question: Which support representatives have the highest number of customers assigned to them?
gold_sql:
WITH SupportRepCustomerCounts AS (
 SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(c.customer_id) AS customer_count
 FROM employee e
 JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
), MaxCount AS (
 SELECT MAX(customer_count) AS max_customer_count FROM SupportRepCustomerCounts
)
SELECT s.employee_id, s.first_name, s.last_name, s.customer_count
FROM SupportRepCustomerCounts s
JOIN MaxCount m ON s.customer_count = m.max_customer_count;

question: Find the support representative who has the highest total invoice value from customers located in California.
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, SUM(i.total) AS total_invoice_value
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE c.state = 'CA'
GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY total_invoice_value DESC
LIMIT 1;

question: Which media types are most commonly used by tracks in the Jazz genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT mt.name AS media_type, COUNT(t.track_id) AS track_count, 
       COUNT(t.track_id) * 1.0 / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM track t2 JOIN genre g2 ON t2.genre_id = g2.genre_id WHERE g2.name = 'Jazz') AS percentage
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
JOIN media_type mt ON t.media_type_id = mt.media_type_id
WHERE g.name = 'Jazz'
GROUP BY mt.name
ORDER BY track_count DESC;

question: Can you get the count of employees who have been hired each year, ordered by hire date?
gold_sql:
SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM hire_date) AS hire_year, COUNT(employee_id) AS employee_count
FROM employee
GROUP BY hire_year
ORDER BY hire_year;

question: What is the average track duration and total number of tracks available for each genre?
gold_sql:
SELECT g.name AS genre_name,
       AVG(t.milliseconds) / 1000.0 AS avg_track_duration_seconds,
       COUNT(t.track_id) AS total_tracks
FROM track t
JOIN genre g ON t.genre_id = g.genre_id
GROUP BY g.name
ORDER BY g.name;

question: Which billing countries contribute most to monthly revenue?
gold_sql:
SELECT
  billing_country,
  TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
  SUM(total) AS monthly_revenue,
  SUM(total) / (SELECT SUM(total) FROM invoice WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')) AS revenue_percentage
FROM invoice
WHERE billing_country IN ('USA', 'Canada', 'Mexico')
GROUP BY billing_country, year_month
ORDER BY year_month, monthly_revenue DESC;

question: Which months in the last year had the highest number of invoices issued, and what was the total revenue for those months?
gold_sql:
SELECT
 TO_CHAR(invoice_date, 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month,
 COUNT(invoice_id) AS invoice_count,
 SUM(total) AS total_revenue
FROM invoice
WHERE invoice_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 year'
 GROUP BY year_month
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: How many tracks are longer than 4 minutes?
Can you list the top 10 artists with the highest total sales from their tracks?
gold_sql:
SELECT a.name AS artist_name, SUM(il.unit_price * il.quantity) AS total_sales
FROM artist a
JOIN album al ON a.artist_id = al.artist_id
JOIN track t ON al.album_id = t.album_id
JOIN invoice_line il ON t.track_id = il.track_id
GROUP BY a.artist_id, a.name
ORDER BY total_sales DESC
LIMIT 10;

question: Which support representatives manage customers with the highest number of invoices issued?
gold_sql:
SELECT e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name, COUNT(i.invoice_id) AS invoice_count
FROM employee e
JOIN customer c ON e.employee_id = c.support_rep_id
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
 GROUP BY e.employee_id, e.first_name, e.last_name
ORDER BY invoice_count DESC;

question: Which customers have invoices with total amounts exceeding the average invoice total across all customers?
gold_sql:
SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, i.total
FROM customer c
JOIN invoice i ON c.customer_id = i.customer_id
WHERE i.total > (SELECT AVG(total) FROM invoice)
